entries.title: Cinematic spawn-on-train intro
entries.summary: You now begin each new world aboard the train. On your first arrival, a short cinematic sweeps up from the ground to reveal your train before handing you the controls — press Space at any time to skip it.
highlights: Spawn standing on a flatbed of the train instead of on the ground beside it
A fly-up intro camera reveals the train the first time you enter a world
Press Space to skip the intro (any other key shows the hint)

entries.title: Auto-publish CurseForge modpack after each release
entries.summary: Dungeon Train now ships as a CurseForge modpack that updates automatically about 15 minutes after every mod release, bundling the matching mod build plus the tested Sable dependency.
highlights: CurseForge modpack auto-follows every release
Bundles the tested Sable version and declares Dungeon Train's dependencies

entries.title: Enemies scale with difficulty level
entries.summary: Train enemies now spawn based on the current difficulty. Each spawn egg has a difficulty range, so the early game stays gentle — small slimes and basic mobs — while tougher foes like husks, bogged and vindicators ramp in only as you travel deeper and the difficulty climbs. When an enemy is out of its range, that spot fills with an in-range enemy instead, so enemy carriages and cages are never left empty.
highlights: Easy mobs (small slimes) are gated to the opening levels; tougher mobs appear only at higher difficulty
Tuned difficulty windows across the skeleton, bogged, pillager, vindicator, zombie and slime carriages
Editors can set a per-egg min/max difficulty band in the block-variant menu (shift-click the max down to 'all')
Slimes are now an early-game threat: small slimes spawn first and taper off as difficulty climbs
Ranged skeletons, husks, bogged, pillager patrols and vindicators ramp in deeper, each on its own difficulty window
Added enemy variety and new spawn spots to the enemies and slime carriages, and re-tuned the caged-zombie spawns

entries.title: Advancements only earnable in survival or adventure
entries.summary: Dungeon Train advancements can no longer be earned in creative or spectator mode — play in survival or adventure to unlock them. The in-game editor's advancements still unlock in creative, and vanilla Minecraft advancements are unaffected.
highlights: DT gameplay advancements can't be earned in creative or spectator
Editor advancements still unlock in creative
Vanilla and other-mod advancements unchanged

entries.title: Carriage spawn-egg mobs arrive armed
entries.summary: Mobs you place in a carriage with spawn eggs now spawn with their normal gear. Pillagers carry a crossbow (and occasionally the raid-captain's ominous banner); skeletons and bogged carry a bow. As the train's difficulty climbs, ranged mobs keep their bow or crossbow and have it enchanted, instead of being handed a melee weapon that left them defenceless at range.
highlights: Spawn-egg pillagers spawn with a crossbow, with a chance at the ominous banner
Spawn-egg skeletons and bogged spawn with a bow
Higher difficulty now enchants a ranged mob's bow/crossbow instead of replacing it

entries.title: Piglins no longer wrongly become magma cubes outside the Nether
entries.summary: On a train's easy opening stretch, tough mobs get swapped for friendly slimes (and Nether creatures for magma cubes). Piglins were being turned into magma cubes even on Overworld trains. Now zombified piglins are never swapped, and piglins and piglin brutes only turn into magma cubes inside the Nether — on other trains they spawn exactly as placed.
highlights: Zombified piglins are never replaced — they always spawn as placed
Piglins & piglin brutes only become magma cubes on Nether trains
Overworld trains no longer spawn out-of-place magma cubes from piglins

entries.title: Compatible Terrain mode + optional Distant Horizons & Tectonic support
entries.summary: A new Compatible Terrain option lets Dungeon Train generate vanilla-style overworlds, so terrain mods like Tectonic and Terralith - and Distant Horizons' long-range views - work with the train. It is off by default (classic Dungeon Train terrain stays the default); switch it on in Dungeon Train settings before creating a world. The modpack now offers Distant Horizons and Tectonic as optional add-ons you can enable at install (use Distant Horizons 2.x).
highlights: New Compatible Terrain option - run the train across Tectonic / Terralith terrain
Distant Horizons + Tectonic offered as opt-in modpack add-ons (never forced)
Classic terrain unchanged by default; existing worlds unaffected

entries.title: Leatherworker shop carriage
entries.summary: A leatherworker's stall now rides the train among the other shops. Its chest is stocked with leather and the full set of leather armour, a whole rainbow of dyes, emeralds, a potion and other odds and ends from the tannery — handy for patching together armour or recolouring your kit mid-run.
highlights: New leatherworker shop carriage joins the train's rotating shops
Stocked with leather, the full leather-armour set and leather horse armour
A complete rainbow of dyes, plus emeralds, a cauldron, a potion and a water bucket

entries.title: Reliable spawn on the train's front deck
entries.summary: Joining or respawning onto the moving train now reliably lands you standing on the front flatbed deck. Before, if the train hadn't fully settled or had already grown past its first carriage, you could occasionally drop through to the tracks under the train or get stuck inside the deck floor.
highlights: Always spawn cleanly on the train's front deck
No more falling under the train or clipping into the flatbed slab on join or respawn

entries.title: Empty enemy spots no longer leave a stray block behind
entries.summary: A carriage spot that can only hold enemies — and whose enemies are all the wrong level for your current depth — now stays as empty air instead of leaving a stray block where a mob would have stood. The enemy still appears once you travel deep enough for it to belong.
highlights: Mob-only carriage spots with no depth-appropriate enemy now clear to air
No more leftover placeholder block where a not-yet-eligible mob would stand

entries.title: Advancements keybind reminder in chat
entries.summary: Earn a Dungeon Train advancement before you've ever opened the advancements screen and a short, train-flavoured message now points you at the key that opens it. It shows your actual (rebindable) keybind, picks at random from a dozen conductor-style lines, and quietly stops for good once you open the advancements screen.
highlights: Chat reminder of the key that opens your advancements
Shows your real, rebindable keybind (not a hardcoded key)
Random from 12 train-flavoured lines; stops once you open the screen

entries.title: Five train-traversal advancements
entries.summary: Five new advancements reward exploring the world the train rides through — hopping down onto the tracks, wandering off and finding your way back aboard, and discovering the bridge-pillar staircases and tunnel stairwells along the route.
highlights: Track Record — set foot on the rails below the train
Off the Rails — leave the train and tracks behind for the open world
Back on Track — climb aboard again after getting off
Taking the Stairs — use a bridge-pillar staircase
Beneath the Candy Kingdom — enter a tunnel stairwell

entries.title: Armor stands as randomized carriage contents
entries.summary: Carriage cells can now randomly feature an armor stand wearing a rolled set of armor, sitting alongside the usual blocks and loot. Some carriages show an equipped stand in a corner, others leave it empty — more variety in what you find aboard the train.
highlights: Armor stands can now appear as random carriage contents, sometimes wearing a full set of gear
Each stand's loadout is rolled per spawn, so no two carriages are guaranteed alike

entries.title: Keep Inventory now carries your gear to the next world
entries.summary: With Keep Inventory turned on, dying and starting a New World now brings your whole inventory and experience along — and the new world keeps the rule on, so your gear and XP carry through run after run. With Keep Inventory off, nothing changes.
highlights: Inventory (including armor and offhand) and XP carry into the next world when Keep Inventory is on
The next world keeps the Keep Inventory rule enabled automatically, so it chains across every run
No change when Keep Inventory is off — the next world still starts empty

entries.title: Quality-of-life mods in the modpack: AppleSkin + Mouse Tweaks
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train modpack now includes AppleSkin — the food saturation and hunger-value overlay — enabled by default (you can untick it when installing). Mouse Tweaks, for faster inventory management (shift-drag and scroll-to-move items), is offered as an optional add-on you can opt into.
highlights: AppleSkin food/saturation overlay now ships with the modpack (on by default)
Mouse Tweaks inventory quality-of-life available as an optional add-on

entries.title: Know The Rules advancement + clearer ride-time goals
entries.summary: A new "Know The Rules" advancement joins the Taking Notes branch — read every Quiet Rule of the train to earn it, and "Respect The Rules" now nests beneath it. Two ride-time advancements also spell out how long you need to stay aboard: "Settling In" (ten hours) and "I Live Here Now" (twenty-four hours).
highlights: New "Know The Rules" advancement — read every Quiet Rule of the train
"Respect The Rules" now sits beneath it in the Taking Notes branch
"Settling In" and "I Live Here Now" descriptions now state the hours required

entries.title: More Quiet Rules of the Train — the maker, the key, and fellow adventurers
entries.summary: The Quiet Rules of the Train book gains ten new entries. Some whisper of reaching the one who built the train; one points to the key that reveals what you came here to do — and shows your own bound advancements key inline; the rest are hard-won advice on the other adventurers you meet: when to bow, when to gift, when to defend, and how to win them over.
highlights: A rule on reaching the train's maker via "@Dev"
A rule that reveals your own advancements key, inline in the page
Seven tips on befriending (or surviving) fellow adventurers

entries.title: Jade tooltips available as an optional modpack add-on
entries.summary: Jade — the "what am I looking at" overlay that shows the name and details of whatever block or item you're aiming at — is now offered as an optional add-on for the Dungeon Train modpack that you can opt into. Heads-up: Jade's tooltip doesn't show for blocks on the moving train yet (a Sable limitation); it works everywhere else.
highlights: Jade block & item tooltips offered as an optional modpack add-on
Known limitation: no tooltip for blocks on the moving train (works fine off-train)

entries.title: Type @Dev in chat: autocomplete + highlight
entries.summary: The bundled Discord Presence mod updates to 0.15.0. Typing @Dev (or @brennanhatton) in chat now autocompletes like a command and turns yellow as you type — so the new Quiet Rule about reaching the maker is something you can actually see and use. The deeper Discord wiring (real pings, engaged-only relay) ships too but stays off until a server owner turns it on.
highlights: @Dev / @brennanhatton autocomplete in chat (Tab to complete) and highlight yellow as you type
Pairs with the new “reach the maker via @Dev” Quiet Rule
Real Discord pings and relay gating are bundled but off by default

entries.title: Meet, befriend, or lay to rest your own echo
entries.summary: Three new advancements follow your reunions with your own echo — a passenger reincarnated from one of your past lives. Walk up to one, win it over with a two-way gift exchange, or put it to rest.
highlights: New "Hello, Old Soul" — come within two blocks of your own echo
"Old Friends, New Life" — befriend your own echo
"Lay to Rest" — kill your own echo

entries.title: AppleSkin listed as an optional dependency
entries.summary: AppleSkin — the food and saturation overlay bundled on-by-default in the Dungeon Train modpack — is now also listed as an optional add-on on the mod's own CurseForge page, so it's discoverable straight from the mod.
highlights: AppleSkin now shown as an optional dependency of the mod

entries.title: Archways fade between elevated track pillars
entries.summary: Where the train's track climbs onto tall support pillars — crossing ravines, rivers, and oceans — the pillars now fade toward each other with stepped archways instead of standing as bare posts. The arches are built from each pillar's own stone, so a viaduct made of fancy or themed pillars arches in its own material.
highlights: Tall track pillars now fade into stepped archways toward their neighbours
Arches copy each pillar's own blocks, so themed pillars arch in-material

entries.title: Everything Burrito: earn every other advancement
entries.summary: A new challenge advancement, Everything Burrito, unlocks once you've earned every other Dungeon Train advancement — editor achievements aside. It hangs off 'Help I Can't Get Off' at the end of the line, and tracks the achievement list dynamically, so it always means '100%' even as new advancements arrive in future updates.
highlights: New 'Everything Burrito' challenge advancement for 100% completion
Counts every non-editor advancement — and auto-includes any added in future updates

entries.title: Pause menu works during the intro cutscene
entries.summary: Switching windows (or pressing Esc) during the spawn intro cutscene now opens a pause menu you can actually use — you can click its buttons and Esc closes it — and the cutscene picks up right where it left off when you come back. Previously the menu could open in a frozen, unclickable state.
highlights: The pause menu is now clickable and closable during the intro cutscene
The intro now pauses and resumes around the menu instead of locking up

entries.title: New decor carriages (house, tree, cherry blossom) + broken roof
entries.summary: A new 'features' content set rides the train — house, tree, and cherry-blossom builds that can appear inside several carriage types, with chests of loot in the tree and cherry-blossom variants. Cracked carriages also gain a new 'broken' roof look.
highlights: New house, tree, and cherry-blossom carriage content (tree & cherry blossom carry loot chests)
New 'broken' roof variant for cracked carriages

entries.title: Fixed players spawning under the train
entries.summary: Spawning or respawning onto the train no longer occasionally drops you through the deck and leaves you stuck underneath. On busy world loads the client could free-fall off the deck before the train's collision finished loading; the game now holds you on the deck through that window so you always start standing on the train.
highlights: No more falling under the train on login, respawn, or world reload

entries.title: Gentler opening: a mob-free start, then slimes, then normal
entries.summary: New runs now ease in over three stages. The first stretch of the train is completely free of hostile mobs, so you can find your feet, meet the villagers and grab starter loot in peace. After that, a stretch of harmless slimes gives you a gentle combat warm-up before the usual mobs and difficulty kick in. It's all on by default and fully tunable per world.
highlights: The first ~10 carriages spawn no hostile mobs — villagers, traders and friendly mobs still ride along
The next ~15 carriages spawn only slimes, for an easy combat warm-up
Normal mobs and the usual difficulty curve resume after the opening stretch
All three stages are on by default and tunable in dungeontrain-server.toml

entries.title: Editor welcome message
entries.summary: Opening the in-game editor now greets you with a short welcome that points out what the editor can do and how to reach Brennan for help.
highlights: A friendly welcome appears in chat a couple of seconds after you first open the editor each session.
Click the @brennanhatton mention to message Brennan directly — your message reaches him on Discord.

entries.title: Tag the dev from in-game chat
entries.summary: Type @dev or @brennanhatton in chat and it now pings the developer in the community Discord — a message shouted into the dark can finally reach someone.
highlights: @dev and @brennanhatton in chat now ping the developer in the community Discord feed

entries.title: Death-screen feedback survey
entries.summary: When you die, a Give Feedback button opens a short survey: rate your run 0-10 with an optional comment, then answer how the difficulty progression feels. Answers post to the community Discord. It walks the questions one at a time and never re-asks one you've already answered.
highlights: Give Feedback button on the death screen
0-10 ratings with optional comments, sent to Discord
Asks how the difficulty progression is feeling

entries.title: Editor welcome shows Brennan's Discord "last seen online"
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train editor welcome can again end with whether Brennan is online on Discord right now, or how long ago he was last seen — so creators know if a quick reply is likely. Powered by Discord Presence's presence query over the community relay; gracefully hidden when his presence is unknown.
highlights: Editor welcome shows "Brennan is online on Discord right now!" or "last seen online X ago"
Gracefully hidden when his Discord presence is unknown

entries.title: Fewer trees in carriage contents
entries.summary: Reduced the tree feature's spawn weight in carriage contents, so trees appear less frequently relative to other decorative features.

entries.title: All advancements now persist across worlds
entries.summary: Every advancement you earn — vanilla Minecraft (Story, Nether, The End, Adventure, Husbandry), Dungeon Train, and other mods — now follows you into every world, the same way Dungeon Train's own advancements always have. Start a brand-new world and your earned advancements are already unlocked, no fanfare. Advancements you'd earned before this update get absorbed automatically the next time you load an existing world. Recipe-book unlocks stay per-world.
highlights: Vanilla and other-mod advancements now carry across worlds, not just Dungeon Train's
Earned advancements from existing worlds are absorbed automatically on next load
Recipe-book unlocks stay per-world

entries.title: Tagging @dev in chat shows how recently Brennan was online
entries.summary: Type @dev or @brennanhatton in chat and the game quietly tells you how recently Brennan was last online on Discord — "online right now" or "online 5 minutes ago" — so you know whether to expect a reply. It only appears when he's been around in the last half hour, lands a beat after your own message, and won't show more than once every 5 minutes.
highlights: Tag @dev or @brennanhatton to see Brennan's recent online status in chat
Only shows when he was online within the last 30 minutes
Quietly rate-limited to once every 5 minutes, so it never spams

entries.title: "Reboarder" advancement now unlocks reliably
entries.summary: Shoving a passenger off the moving train now reliably earns the Reboarder advancement — including when they tumble off the open side of a carriage into a gap or fall onto the tracks below, not just when they're knocked cleanly clear. Those off-the-edge falls used to slip through and never counted.
highlights: Pushing a passenger off the train now reliably unlocks Reboarder
Counts passengers that fall off the side or onto the tracks, not only clean knock-offs

entries.title: New book: "All the Creator Asks"
entries.summary: A new random book joins the pool — a fallen adventurer's reverent testament that treats the death-screen feedback survey as a holy rite of the afterlife, written to gently beg you to answer it. Eight short variants turn up in chests, on bookshelves, and at narrative lecterns.
highlights: New 8-variant random book that venerates the feedback survey as a spiritual passage
Found in chest rolls, bookshelf drops, and narrative lecterns

entries.title: Replay the intro cinematic on demand
entries.summary: The opening cinematic is no longer a one-time thing — run /dungeontrain cinematic (or /dt cinematic) any time to watch it again. By default the camera lifts off from a spot on the ground beside the track, right where you are, and sweeps up and away as the train carries you onward — just like the intro. Prefer to start from exactly where you're looking? Use /dt cinematic current.
highlights: New /dt cinematic command replays the opening cinematic on demand
Default shot rises from the ground beside you near the track; 'current' starts from your current view

entries.title: Carriage # and difficulty on Discord advancement posts
entries.summary: Earn an advancement and the community Discord now shows which carriage you were riding and your difficulty level at that moment, posted on its own line right below the advancement.
highlights: Reads e.g. "Carriage +7 · Difficulty Level 3", matching the in-game HUD.

entries.title: New advancement: The Great Beyond
entries.summary: Earn a new advancement — "The Great Beyond" — by completing the death-screen feedback survey. Once you've answered every survey question, it unlocks. The survey is provided by the bundled Discord Presence mod, which now also lets you open it any time with the new /feedback command.
highlights: "The Great Beyond" — Complete the feedback survey after death.
Counts however you finish the survey — on the death screen or via /feedback.

entries.title: Time-aboard & distance-ridden advancements
entries.summary: Seven new advancements reward the long haul. Stay aboard for an hour or two without dying, and rack up the kilometres you ride — both within a single life and across every run you've ever taken. They branch off Dungeon Train Explorer in the advancements screen.
highlights: Single-life time aboard: One Long Ride (1 hour) and Reality Escaped (2 hours)
Single-life distance: Escaping Spawn (10 km), Cross-Country (100 km), The Long Run (250 km)
Lifetime distance: Covering ground (100 km) and I got so far (1,000 km)

entries.title: Narrative death screen
entries.summary: Dying on the train now opens a paginated, narrated retrospective instead of the plain vanilla screen. The Dungeon Train reflects on your fall, your deeds, the cargo you carried, all your lives, and the platform ahead — your run and lifetime stats woven in, a train that fills as you read, and lore that adapts to how you died. Page through with Continue, then Board anew or Leave the line.
highlights: Paginated narrative death screen with data-driven lore
Per-run and lifetime stats: carriages, distance, time aboard, mobs, friends, loot containers, books, deaths
A train graphic that fills carriage by carriage as you advance
Optional end-of-run feedback survey, then Board anew or Leave the line

entries.title: Disable vanilla movement tutorial on world creation
entries.summary: The vanilla Minecraft movement tutorial (WASD / look around / jump hints in the top-right corner) no longer appears when starting a new Dungeon Train world. These hints are irrelevant in DT and conflicted with the cinematic intro.
highlights: Vanilla WASD / look / jump tutorial hints no longer appear on world start

entries.title: TrainCinematographer mode
entries.summary: New spectator mode for filming dungeon runs. Enter with /dungeontrain cinematographer; nearby doors on the train auto-open as you approach. Press C to replay the cinematic intro. The DT branch HUD hides automatically while active.

entries.title: Cinematographer mode only opens doors now
entries.summary: The spectator filming mode added in 0.316.0 was also swinging open trapdoors, fence gates and barrels as the camera drifted past. It now opens only actual doors, so set dressing and storage stay put while you film.
highlights: Trapdoors, fence gates and barrels no longer flip open in cinematographer mode
Only real doors auto-open as the camera approaches

entries.title: Persistent Ender Chest across runs
entries.summary: The Ender Chest now persists between worlds — items you store survive your death and carry into the next run. Survival and creative inventories are kept separate, so switching game modes gives you a fresh Ender Chest for that mode.
highlights: Ender Chest contents carry forward when you die and start a new world
Separate inventory per game mode (survival, creative, adventure, spectator)
Swaps live when you switch game modes mid-session

entries.title: Death screen: lectern stories count as books, plus first-death send-off
entries.summary: Stories you read at narrative lecterns now count toward the "Books read" tally on your death-screen run summary — previously only books read from your hand were counted. And your very first death now gets its own bespoke lines on the "all your lives" and "the platform" pages, instead of the generic send-off written for veteran riders.
highlights: Narrative-lectern stories now count toward "Books read" on the death screen
Unique first-death dialogue on the "all your lives" and "the platform" pages

entries.title: Open-ended feedback survey question
entries.summary: After you die, the feedback survey's second question is now open-ended — "If you could change one thing about the mod, what would it be?" — answered in a free-text box instead of a 0–10 rating. The first question (how likely you'd recommend the game) is unchanged, and your answer is only sent if you actually type something.
highlights: Survey's second question is now a free-text "what would you change?" prompt
Replaces the old difficulty-progression 0–10 rating

entries.title: Cinematographer clear-view: see through walls to film the train
entries.summary: TrainCinematographer mode gains a clear-view sub-mode: while filming, the blocks directly in front of the camera turn invisible so terrain, carriage walls and clutter stop blocking the shot — then quietly return as you pan away, leaving the world untouched. Doors, trapdoors and slabs are left alone, and the camera's automatic door-opening now reaches much further down the train.
highlights: New: /dt cinematographer clearview on|off — see straight through whatever the camera is pointed at
Blocks turn invisible (not removed), so nothing collapses and your train stays intact — decorated pots, bookshelves and chests come back with their contents
Cinematographer now auto-opens doors much further ahead (50 blocks)

entries.title: Ender Chest Persistence extracted to standalone mod
entries.summary: The Ender Chest cross-world and per-game-mode persistence is now shipped as the standalone Ender Chest Persistence mod (bundled in DT's jar, so no separate install is needed). The player-facing behaviour is identical — Ender Chest contents are preserved across world resets and isolated by game mode.

entries.title: Photos of your ride behind the death screen
entries.summary: The train now quietly snaps third-person photos of your journey and shows them as the backdrop on the death screen — a recap of your run, with the most fitting shot behind each page.
highlights: Photos are taken throughout the ride and become the background on each death-screen page
Shots favour well-lit moments where you're in clear view, and capture key beats — fights, new armour, opening chests, breaking pots, reading lore, and meeting travellers
Optional chat log of when each photo is taken — toggle it in the X menu under Options

entries.title: Tougher zombie rooms + book carriage refresh
entries.summary: Zombie carriages now spawn more zombies and are lit with torches in the corners, and the book carriage's room layout has been refreshed.
highlights: Zombie rooms spawn more zombies, now torch-lit
Refreshed book carriage layout

entries.title: Death-screen ride photos only snap when you're in clear view
entries.summary: Ride photos now confirm there's an unobstructed line to your character before snapping, so a death-screen background never shows you hidden behind a carriage wall. Inside walled carriages the camera finds an angle that actually sees you — down an aisle or through a doorway — or skips the shot until one opens up.
highlights: No more death-screen photos of the back of a wall — every shot has a clear view of you
Works inside Sable carriages, not just open decks: the camera checks the carriage's own walls
If nothing can see you right now, the photo is skipped and retried instead of captured blind

entries.title: FerriteCore added to the modpack for lower memory use
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train modpack now bundles FerriteCore as an on-by-default add-on that quietly trims memory use. It deduplicates internal game data (block states, models) to cut RAM on this content-heavy pack, with no change to how the game plays. You can untick it during install.
highlights: On-by-default Include — deselectable at install; the standalone mod is unaffected.
Measured ~18% lower client memory at the menu; safe with the moving train (Sable).
Full memory benchmark and methodology: [wiki → Compatibility → FerriteCore](https://github.com/bh679/dungeon-train-mc/wiki/Compatibility#ferritecore)

entries.title: Cause of death on the fall screen
entries.summary: The death screen's first page now tells you what actually ended the run. Its title shows your cause of death in the second person — "You fell from a high place", "You were slain by a zombie", "You blew up" — instead of the generic "the fall".
highlights: Fall-page title now names how you died
Reads in second person ("You fell from a high place", "You were blown up by a creeper")

entries.title: Biome-diversity achievements
entries.summary: Ride the Dungeon Train through a variety of biomes in a single life to earn four new achievements — from your first handful of biomes all the way to seeing every kind of terrain the world has to offer.
highlights: Far Afield, Many Lands & World Without End — ride through 10, 17 and 25 different biomes in a single life
Terra Omnia — visit all 8 biome families (forest, frozen, jungle, desert, savanna, mountain, wetland and ocean) in one life
A 'Biome family discovered (N/8)' message keeps track as you roll into each new kind of terrain

entries.title: Ride photos paced across your journey, with no repeats on the death screen
entries.summary: Ride photos no longer bunch up at the start of a run. The first photo of each kind is snapped as soon as it happens, then each kind spaces out the further you travel. The death screen also no longer shows the same photo on more than one page.
highlights: First scenic/combat/gear/lore/social photo is taken as soon as it happens, then spaces out as you travel
Each death-screen page shows a different photo where enough were captured

entries.title: Modpack: ModernFix bundled by default (faster launch, less memory)
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train CurseForge modpack now ships ModernFix as an on-by-default add-on, alongside AppleSkin and FerriteCore. It speeds up launch and world-load times and trims memory use — a welcome win on a heavy pack like Dungeon Train. It's optional: untick it at install if you'd rather not. (Modpack only — the mod jar is unchanged; ModernFix is also now listed as an optional companion on the mod page.)
highlights: ModernFix added to the modpack, on by default (deselectable at install)
Faster launch + world-load and lower memory; verified safe alongside Sable
[Compatibility + launch/memory A/B test results](https://github.com/bh679/dungeon-train-mc/wiki/Compatibility#modernfix)

entries.title: Wire Ender Chest Persistence as embedded dependency in release metadata
entries.summary: CurseForge and Modrinth now show the ender-chest-persistence mod as embedded inside Dungeon Train, matching the existing AIN/AIS/PMOB declarations.

entries.title: Ride photos wait for a smooth moment
entries.summary: Ride snapshots now only fire when your game has the headroom to spare, so the camera no longer hitches a frame while you're already lagging. If performance stays rough for a while, the next time you open a menu it quietly grabs one so your death screen still gets its backdrops.
highlights: Capture pauses when FPS — or single-player TPS — drops below a threshold, and resumes on its own once it recovers.
After a long rough patch, opening your inventory, chat, or a chest takes a catch-up photo.
Thresholds live in the client config (rideSnapshots.minFps / minTps); set either to 0 to turn that check off.

entries.title: Death-screen photos never repeat two pages in a row
entries.summary: On a short run that didn't capture enough ride photos to give every death-screen page its own, the screen could show the same photo on two pages back-to-back. Now the photos are spread out so consecutive pages always differ — you'll only see a photo twice in a row if the whole run produced a single photo.
highlights: Scarce photos are alternated across the death pages instead of doubling up side by side
Each page still favours its fitting photo where that doesn't cause a back-to-back repeat

entries.title: Portrait of the traveller you befriended or killed, on the death screen
entries.summary: The death screen's deeds page now shows an animated, full-body portrait of a fellow traveller from your run — the player-mob you befriended (on the left), or, if you made no friends, the one you killed (on the right). It's shown with their name and the gear they were carrying. Friendly travellers glance around and crouch-bob when they catch your eye; ones you killed stare you down, swing their weapon, and raise their shield. "Friends" now means anyone who grew to like you, and the friends tally counts them all.
highlights: Animated player-mob portrait on the deeds page — befriended (left) or killed (right)
Rendered full-body with the traveller's name and the gear they last carried
Friends look around and crouch-bob at you; foes stare, swing, and block
The friends tally now counts every traveller who grew to like you

entries.title: Cinematic death-screen page transitions
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train's death screen now turns its pages with a slow fade through black. The text and dark overlay fade out, the next page's text fades in, and the ride photo behind it rises gently up out of black while you read. Each settled page also sits a little darker so the narration stays clear over the photography. Press Continue whenever you're ready and the photo finishes revealing as the page turns.
highlights: Pages cross-fade through black instead of hard-cutting, the next ride photo rising slowly out of the dark.
Settled pages are a touch darker so the narrative text is easier to read over the ride photos.
Press Continue or Space whenever you like; if the photo is still rising in, it finishes as the page turns.

entries.title: Quieter biome exploration
entries.summary: Riding into a new biome family no longer pops a "Biome family discovered" message on screen. The exploration advancements still track and award exactly as before — only the on-screen notification is gone.

entries.title: Over the Top advancement
entries.summary: New advancement fires when a player runs across the roof of one carriage group without dropping inside — sits before Skip the Grind in the tree.

entries.title: Free Play for creative & cheat runs
entries.summary: Playing in creative or spectator, or using cheat commands, no longer blocks advancements — you keep earning them live. The run simply becomes Free Play: you're asked to confirm first (with a don't-show-again option), a spyglass effect marks the run, and anything you earn while it's on won't be saved to your profile or count toward your global stats. Start a new world for a fresh, ranked run.
highlights: Creative, spectator, cinematographer and cheat commands start Free Play instead of silently disabling advancements.
A confirmation lets you back out before Free Play takes effect, with a Don't-show-again option.
Free Play is marked by a permanent spyglass status effect — hover it in your inventory to see what it means.
Fixed a blank final page on the death screen after dying in Creative.

entries.title: Death screen Space/Enter hotkeys removed
entries.summary: Space and Enter no longer advance the death screen — click Continue to proceed

entries.title: Advancement hints replace the ??? tooltip
entries.summary: Haven't earned an advancement yet? Its tooltip now shows a hint or riddle nudging you toward how to unlock it, instead of a blank "???". Covers 48 Dungeon Train advancements across riding, reading, exploring and befriending — a handful of the most spoiler-heavy ones stay a mystery on purpose.
highlights: Unearned advancements now show a hint instead of "???"
48 advancements hinted across the Dungeon Train tab
Wiki Advancements page now lists every advancement with its hint

entries.title: Pause-menu buttons usable during the intro cinematic
entries.summary: Opening the pause menu during the spawn intro cinematic and clicking Options, Advancements, Statistics, Feedback, or Report Bugs now works. Previously those buttons did nothing until the cinematic ended — only Return to Game and Save & Quit responded.
highlights: Pause-menu buttons (Options, Advancements, Statistics, …) now work while the intro cinematic plays

entries.title: Save your ride photos from the death screen
entries.summary: The death screen now has a photos button on its final page that opens a gallery of every ride photo taken during the run. Browse them all and save the ones you love to your screenshots folder before the line carries you on.
highlights: New photos button on the death screen's final page
Browse all of the run's ride photos in one gallery
Save any photo (or all of them) to your screenshots folder, plus an Open folder shortcut

entries.title: Death-screen feedback survey is now skippable
entries.summary: The feedback survey on the death screen is optional again — the Continue button now advances past survey questions whether or not you answer them. Answering still submits your feedback as before.

entries.title: Uncraftable arrows now pack an escalating punch
entries.summary: The effectless "Uncraftable" tipped arrows you scavenge from vases and chests now carry a real offensive potion — and it grows nastier the deeper you ride. Every arrow you find within a 50-carriage stretch shares the same effect, then it steps up at the next stretch: Weakness early on, ramping through Slowness, Poison, and Harming as you travel further from the start. Loot containers you place yourself scale to how far you've travelled, too.
highlights: Uncraftable tipped arrows now apply an offensive effect when fired
The effect stays the same across each 50-carriage stretch, then escalates at the next one
Ramps Weakness -> Slowness -> Poison -> Harming -> Poison II -> Harming II the deeper you travel
Loot containers you place by hand scale to your own travelled distance

entries.title: Ride photos use less memory on long runs
entries.summary: The third-person photos taken during your run (the ones shown behind the death screen) now quietly save to disk when your game has performance to spare and you're sitting in a menu, freeing up memory while you ride. The death screen and the photo gallery look and work exactly the same.
highlights: Photos offload to disk only during quiet moments (high frame rate and a menu open), so there's no in-game stutter
Frees memory on long runs; the death screen and photo gallery are unchanged
Saving a photo from the gallery reuses the offloaded file, so it's instant

entries.title: Discord chat-relay consent card + /chatconnect
entries.summary: Updated the bundled Discord Presence integration to its latest version. A new card on the title screen explains the optional Discord chat relay and lets you choose whether your in-game chat is shared, and a new /chatconnect command lets you toggle it anytime while playing.
highlights: New title-screen card explains the optional Discord chat relay and lets you opt in or out
New /chatconnect command toggles the Discord chat relay in-game

entries.title: Advancements on the death-screen cargo page
entries.summary: The death screen's cargo page now shows the Dungeon Train advancements you earned this life — each in its real advancement frame — and moves the loot-container and book tallies onto the gear row as chest and book icons. A button opens the full advancements screen.
highlights: Advancements earned this life, shown on the cargo page in their real per-tier advancement frames
Loot containers and books read now appear as chest and book icons on the gear row (hover for labels)
Scrolls when you've earned a lot, plus a button to view all advancements

entries.title: Modpack: Advancement Plaques on by default, plus more one-click add-ons
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train modpack now ships Advancement Plaques enabled by default, so your hard-won advancements pop up as a fancy animated plaque instead of the plain vanilla toast. AppleSkin, FerriteCore and ModernFix are now correctly switched on out of the box too, and Mouse Tweaks, Jade, Distant Horizons and Tectonic come bundled and ready to toggle on whenever you want them.
highlights: Advancement Plaques enabled by default — fancier advancement popups
AppleSkin, FerriteCore & ModernFix now correctly on by default
Mouse Tweaks, Jade, Distant Horizons (2.x) & Tectonic bundled as one-click opt-ins

entries.title: Sable physics updated to 2.0.2
entries.summary: Updated the Sable physics dependency from 1.2.1 to 2.0.2. Server tick-time is equal-or-better — worst-case lag spikes are roughly halved — and the long-standing save-and-quit hang (Sable #679) no longer occurs. Verified API-compatible, so no gameplay changes. Full compatibility & benchmark notes: [Compatibility wiki](https://github.com/bh679/dungeon-train-mc/wiki/Compatibility#sable-202mc1211).
highlights: Smoother performance — roughly 50% fewer worst-case server lag spikes
Fixed the save-and-quit hang affecting some worlds (Sable #679)
Sable physics updated to 2.0.2

entries.title: PlayerMobs follow you on the train, echoes wear real skins
entries.summary: Updated the bundled Interactive PlayerMobs to its latest version. A PlayerMob that has come to love you now leaves its own route to make its way to your carriage and rides along with you, and reincarnated "Echo of <name>" mobs now wear the real skin of the player they came from. The skin pool also grew to 68 with 30 new recognizable player skins.
highlights: Loved PlayerMobs leave their route to ride in your carriage (toggle: trainFollowLovedPlayer, on by default)
Reincarnated echoes now wear the real player's skin instead of a default Steve/Alex
30 new player skins (pool now 68); recognizable skins appear ~40% of the time

entries.title: Modpack: AmbientSounds on by default
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train modpack now bundles AmbientSounds — an immersive ambient/environmental sound engine — enabled by default, along with its CreativeCore library. Client-side audio only; no effect on the train or Sable physics.
highlights: Richer ambient & environmental audio out of the box
Bundled with its required CreativeCore library

entries.title: Update 'Collecting Stories' hint
entries.summary: Changed the hint text to 'The lecterns hold the key.'

entries.title: Breaking a narrative lectern drops a lectern and its book
entries.summary: Mining a narrative lectern now gives you a normal lectern instead of a non-craftable special block, and the story book that was on it pops out too — so you can pick it back up and keep reading.
highlights: Narrative lecterns drop a plain lectern when broken
The story book on the lectern now drops as well

entries.title: Echo lore books
entries.summary: Added two new echo-themed variants to Quiet Rules of the Train, and two new echo/past-life observations to Adventurer's Field Notes.

entries.title: Stray command block removed from the vindicator carriage
entries.summary: An enemy (vindicator) carriage could spawn a leftover command block sitting on the floor where a raider belongs. The marker is now cleared, so the cell stays clean and the mob spawns as intended.

entries.title: More community names in the item-name pool
entries.summary: Updated the bundled Adventure Item Names mod to 0.44.0, adding JJ, Freshboii, VidTu, DanMizu, and Varocraft25 to the pool of Discord community members who can appear as item-name contributors.

entries.title: Adventurer's Field Notes — passenger observation
entries.summary: New field notes entry: an anonymous adventurer wonders whether the passengers are truly alive, and shares that food, armour, or tools are the fastest way to make allies.

entries.title: Five new quality-of-life companion mods in the modpack
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train modpack now bundles a set of client-side quality-of-life and cosmetic companions: a searchable controls menu (Controlling), side-by-side gear comparison tooltips (Equipment Compare), a highlight on freshly picked-up inventory items (Item Highlighter), and an on-screen item pickup notifier (Pick Up Notifier) — all enabled out of the box. Smooth Swapping (animated inventory item movement) is bundled as an opt-in extra.
highlights: Searchable controls/key-binds screen (Controlling)
Hold-Shift gear comparison tooltips (Equipment Compare)
New-item highlight in your inventory (Item Highlighter)
On-screen item pickup notifications (Pick Up Notifier)
Optional animated inventory swapping (Smooth Swapping, off by default)

entries.title: Useless loot potions now brew up a real, escalating effect
entries.summary: The blank, effectless potions you used to scavenge — the 'Uncraftable' bottles and plain water that did nothing — now come brewed with a real effect, and they grow more potent the deeper you ride. How strong an effect you find is tied to how far you've travelled: gentle brews early on, ramping up to powerful concoctions later. But each bottle is its own surprise — it might be a drink, a splash, or a lingering cloud, carrying any effect from the current tier, so two potions found in the same stretch can be completely different. Loot containers you place yourself scale to how far you've travelled, too.
highlights: Effectless 'Uncraftable' potions in loot now carry a real effect
Effects grow stronger the further you travel — gentle early, powerful later
Every bottle is its own roll: a mix of effects, and drinkable / splash / lingering forms
Water, awkward and other no-effect potions get upgraded too; real potions are left untouched
Loot containers you place by hand scale to your own travelled distance

entries.title: "The Far Start" advancement — carry your first book past 150 carriages
entries.summary: A new challenge advancement, "The Far Start": keep the unread starting book that drops at spawn and carry it past 150 carriages in a single life to earn it. Reading the book burns it, so this rewards hauling it the long way without ever opening it.
highlights: New "The Far Start" challenge advancement (child of "Dungeon Train Explorer")
Earned by carrying the starting book beyond 150 carriages in one life

entries.title: Free Play protects your real Ender Chest
entries.summary: When a run switches to Free Play — by going creative or spectator, or using cheat commands — your Ender Chest now swaps to a separate Creative one for the rest of that run. Cheated items can never end up in the Ender Chest you use on your legit survival runs, and a Free Play run can't peek into or change your real chest. Your saved items stay exactly as you left them.
highlights: Free Play runs get their own separate Creative Ender Chest
Your real Ender Chest is never read or written during a cheated run
Swaps the instant a run becomes Free Play, and stays put across relogs

entries.title: Dev/test builds send Discord to a dev channel, not the live feed
entries.summary: Development and test builds of the mod now route their Discord activity (joins, deaths, chat, advancements) to a separate dev channel instead of the live community feed, so testing no longer adds noise for players. Released builds are unchanged — they post to the community feed exactly as before.
highlights: Dev/test builds post Discord to a separate dev channel
Released builds unchanged — still post to the live community feed

entries.title: Free Play activity now shows in Discord
entries.summary: When a player's run enters Free Play (creative, spectator, or cheat commands), Discord now gets a heads-up. Free Play runs also keep posting their death and "left the game" run summaries to Discord, so the community feed stays complete — cross-world stats still stay frozen in Free Play.
highlights: Discord notice the moment a run enters Free Play
Death and "left the game" summaries now post for Free Play runs too
New freePlayNoticeToDiscord config toggle (on by default)

entries.title: Custom network-access card + Discord Presence 0.31.0
entries.summary: The title-screen "use the internet?" prompt is now Dungeon Train's own card spelling out exactly what the connection is used for, and the bundled Discord integration was updated to 0.31.0 with a bot-token security fix.
highlights: The title-screen network-access prompt is now Dungeon Train's own card — leaderboard scoring (coming soon), dev support chat, and a heads-up that a future update will let you return as a mob "echo" in other players' worlds.
Toggle network features anytime with /chatconnect on|off (works even with cheats off).
Bundled Discord Presence 0.31.0 — the Discord bot token now stays server-side only (security fix).

entries.title: PlayerMobs can now turn up behind you on the train
entries.summary: Each time a PlayerMob spawns on the train, there is now a small chance another one also appears a short way behind you and walks your direction of travel, catching up from the rear. It is not hostile; it just shows up behind you. Adjust it with the new 'Behind %' setting (default 15%, 0 to disable).
highlights: New 'Behind %' spawn chance (default 15%) with a per-world override in the in-game settings screen.

entries.title: Abandon This Run replaces Save and Quit on the pause menu
entries.summary: The pause menu now leads with a red "Abandon This Run" button: one click ends your run and takes you straight to the death recap — no quiet save-and-quit. The usual exits are still there, tucked behind Shift: hold Shift and the red button splits into "Exit to Title" and "Quit Game". Singleplayer only; on servers the normal Disconnect button is unchanged.
highlights: Red "Abandon This Run" button ends the run and opens the death screen
Hold Shift to reveal "Exit to Title" and "Quit Game"
Both exits save the world cleanly (no Sable save-quit hang)

entries.title: Arrival death-photo
entries.summary: The death screen now always opens on a shot of where your run began: a photo of you on the train is captured as soon as you arrive — once the carriages around you have rendered in — so even a quick death has a memento of the start of the journey.
highlights: A photo of your arrival on the train is now guaranteed for the death screen
Waits for the surrounding carriages to load so the shot frames the train, not empty space

entries.title: Themed cause of death when you abandon a run
entries.summary: Abandoning a run from the pause menu now ends it with a themed Dungeon Train cause of death instead of a plain "You died". One of four lines is picked at random each time — "You abandoned the train", "You stepped off the train", "You left the line", or "You walked away from the line" — and it shows on your death screen (and in the Discord run report).
highlights: Abandoning a run now reads as a themed cause of death, not "You died"
Four flavor lines, picked at random each time

entries.title: The internet prompt now says what it won't do
entries.summary: The first-launch "Connect to the internet?" prompt now lists a reassurance alongside the features — marked with a red ✗ — clarifying one thing the connection will never do. Rest easy: it will not harvest your soul.

entries.title: More to read at the end of the line
entries.summary: The death screen's narration has more variety. New lines across all five pages — the fall, the deeds, the cargo, all your lives, and the platform — so back-to-back deaths read differently, including lines that react to a friendless run, a heavy kill count, an empty or overflowing pack, and how many times you've ridden.
highlights: Many new narration variants on every death-screen page
Some lines react to your run: no friends made, 5+ slain, an empty or 250+-loot pack, books read, and your total deaths

entries.title: Cross-world reincarnation — passengers return as echoes from other worlds
entries.summary: Player-mob passengers on the train can now return as "echoes" of people who died in other worlds — not just your own past lives. Every death is shared to a community-wide pool, and a passenger riding near your depth may come back carrying someone else's final loadout and story.
highlights: Passengers can spawn as remote echoes of players from other worlds, not only your own past lives
Each death you suffer joins a shared cross-world pool that feeds everyone's trains
Echoes are drawn from deaths near your current carriage depth, favouring recent ones

entries.title: Death-screen button now reads "Next Screen"
entries.summary: The button that pages through your end-of-run recap on the death screen now reads "Next Screen" instead of "Continue", making it clearer that it steps through the recap rather than dismissing it.
highlights: Death-screen page button relabeled "Continue" → "Next Screen"

entries.title: Deaths now share to a public Discord channel
entries.summary: Death reports now post to a dedicated public Discord deaths channel — the redesigned 'manifest' report, with your run's ride photo and the train's account of how it ended. Free Play (creative / cheats) runs are kept out of the public feed.
highlights: Legit deaths post the full manifest report (ride photo + narrated run) to the public deaths channel
Free Play / creative runs are excluded from the public feed

entries.title: Public death reports show your ride photo
entries.summary: Death reports in the public Discord channel now use a scenic photo from your run as the image, as intended — released builds were falling back to your gear composite.

entries.title: Hold Shift on the death screen to quit straight to desktop
entries.summary: On the death screen, hold Shift to turn the "leave the line" controls into a "Quit Game" button that exits Minecraft straight to desktop — no need to stop at the title screen first. Mirrors the pause menu's Shift-to-quit shortcut, and restyles darker with lighter text so it's clearly a different action.
highlights: Hold Shift on the death screen to swap "Leave the line" for "Quit Game" (quits to desktop)
Works on both the always-visible top-bar chip and the final platform-page button

entries.title: Dungeon Train modpack now on Modrinth
entries.summary: The curated Dungeon Train modpack — the mod plus Sable and its recommended companions — now publishes to Modrinth alongside CurseForge, so you can install it straight from the Modrinth App. It ships the same mods at the same versions as the CurseForge pack.
highlights: Install the full Dungeon Train pack from the Modrinth App
Same mods and versions as the CurseForge pack

entries.title: Echo encounter stories on Discord
entries.summary: When you cross paths with a remote echo — a traveller who died in another world, now walking your train — the game quietly chronicles the meeting and shares the tale to Discord when it ends.
highlights: Tracks the whole encounter: meeting, eye contact, a crouched greeting, blows traded, gifts exchanged, a shove off the train, and the final parting.
Each story names who the echo was and how your paths crossed; only remote (cross-world) echoes are chronicled.

entries.title: Cleaner, wider passenger-log death report
entries.summary: The public death report in #passenger-log has a cleaner, wider layout. Your run's story stays at the top, the key stats move into tidy side-by-side columns, and any advancements you earned get their own row above the ride photo.
highlights: Run stats now sit in side-by-side columns: The fall, This run, and The souls
New 'The souls' column — other souls met, befriended, and slain this run
Advancements earned this run show on their own row beneath the columns
The story now shows what mattered most that run — your fights and friendships, or your haul
Shorter and easier to read at a glance

entries.title: Echo encounter stories now include a photo of the echo
entries.summary: When the game shares the tale of your meeting with a remote echo on Discord, it now adds a snapshot of that traveller — framed against the train and captured the moment you first lay eyes on one another.
highlights: Each echo encounter story now carries a photo of the echo, taken at first eye contact.
Quietly falls back to the text-only story when a clear shot can't be grabbed in time.

entries.title: Backward train generation no longer stalls
entries.summary: Walking toward the back of a moving train used to hit a dead end where new carriages stopped appearing — most noticeably when travelling slowly. The trailing carriages are now kept loaded long enough to lock into place, so the train keeps building behind you for as far as you care to walk.
highlights: The train now keeps generating carriages as you travel toward the rear
Fixes the missing 'end of train' when moving slowly backward

entries.title: Short abandoned runs stay off the public death feed
entries.summary: Bailing out of a run early no longer broadcasts it. If you hit "Abandon This Run" before reaching 20 carriages, your run is kept out of the public death feed — only runs that actually went somewhere get posted. Your own run summary and death recap are unchanged.
highlights: Abandoned runs under 20 carriages are no longer posted to the public feed
Longer abandoned runs and all normal deaths still post as before

entries.title: Echo encounter stories now post to the public death feed
entries.summary: The remote-echo encounter story now appears in the public death-report channel alongside the death manifests, instead of the live community feed — keeping its distinct greyish-blue colour so it still reads apart from a death.
highlights: Encounter stories moved to the public death-report channel, next to the death manifests.
Keeps the greyish-blue embed bar so they stay visually distinct from deaths.

entries.title: Spider and Husk/Skeleton enemy carriages
entries.summary: Two new enemy carriage types join the train: a spider carriage and a husk/skeleton carriage, adding more variety to the enemies you'll face.

entries.title: New story: Tomas Aaro, "The Walking Father"
entries.summary: A new findable story rides the train: Tomas Aaro, "The Walking Father." A farmer leaves his dying village chasing a rumor that the train holds "what you need," and the endless carriages take their toll. The third of the Aaro stories, completing the family begun by Pip and Della.
highlights: New findable story: "The Walking Father" (6 letters, branching variants)
Completes the Aaro family stories — Pip, Della, and now Tomas

entries.title: A creator's plea on the second feedback question
entries.summary: When the death-screen feedback survey reaches its second question, the calm 'ledger' welcome gives way to an urgent, heartfelt plea from the line's creator to keep the railway alive — drawn at random from seven variations so it lands a little differently each run.
highlights: The second feedback question now has its own message, distinct from the first
Seven randomized creator pleas — one chosen per death

entries.title: Feedback reaches the developer instantly
entries.summary: Every feedback-survey answer you submit now pings the developer privately in Discord, so your suggestions are seen right away. The Free Play notice no longer pings.

entries.title: CurseForge changelogs now show real release notes
entries.summary: CurseForge pages for the mod and modpack now display each update's real release notes instead of a bare GitHub link or a generic placeholder. CurseForge only shows the newest file's changelog, and the steady stream of automated bundle updates kept pushing the real notes out of view — they're now carried forward so they stay visible.
highlights: Curated release notes now appear on the CurseForge mod and modpack Changelog tab

entries.title: Discord integration update
entries.summary: Behind-the-scenes update to the Discord join message.

entries.title: Seamless train when you travel toward the back
entries.summary: Walking toward the rear of a moving train no longer opens permanent gaps between carriages or hits a dead-end where the train just stops — carriages now keep generating smoothly behind you, the same way they do ahead. Also fixes a rare crash (and save corruption) that could happen as far-back carriages were unloaded and reloaded.
highlights: No more permanent voids between carriages when riding toward the tail.
The train no longer dead-ends behind you — backward carriages keep coming.
Fixed a rare crash / save corruption from carriage unload-and-reload.

entries.title: The world breaks apart — repeating Void and End-island bands
entries.summary: As your train rolls on, the world starts coming apart. Solid ground crumbles into empty void, then reforms as floating End islands — chorus plants and all — before dissolving back to void and rebuilding into normal terrain. The whole cycle repeats forever down the line. Over the broken stretches the sky fades to the End's starfield and the fog goes dark, only endermen roam, and the train rides straight through on its tracks.
highlights: The world now breaks apart into repeating Void → End-island → Void bands as you travel
Real floating End islands with chorus plants drift in the void; the sky, fog and clouds turn to the End
Only endermen spawn in the broken bands — the train keeps rolling on its rails the whole way

entries.title: Complete Della and Soren Aaro stories
entries.summary: Ships 'The Searching Mother' (Pip's Mum) and 'The Keeper of the Door' (Soren) in full. All three Aaro family stories — Pip, Della, and Tomas — are now complete.
highlights: Della Aaro's 'The Searching Mother' — all 7 letters
Soren's 'The Keeper of the Door' — all 7 letters
Tomas Aaro's 'The Walking Father' was already shipped in v0.347.0

entries.title: Smoother void sky transitions
entries.summary: As the world breaks apart into the void, the sky now holds onto its overworld look a little longer before fading to the End — and on the way out, the sky clears back to normal before solid ground returns beneath you.
highlights: Sky fade lags the crumbling terrain on both edges for a less abrupt transition
Tunable via the disintegrationSkyFadeOffsetBlocks config (0 = old synced behaviour)

entries.title: A longer, more dramatic disintegration journey
entries.summary: The stretch where the world falls apart now runs much further before it reforms. You travel roughly 5,000 blocks of normal overworld before the ground erodes into a void, cross floating End islands stretching about 5,000 blocks under the End sky, fall back through void, and then ride a full 10,000 blocks of overworld before the whole cycle begins again.
highlights: ~5,000 blocks of overworld before the first void (previously 500)
~5,000 blocks of floating End islands at the heart of the band (previously 500)
10,000 blocks of overworld between repeats of the band (previously 500)
All phase lengths are configurable, including a new setting for how long the first overworld lasts

entries.title: The End's soundtrack across the disintegration void
entries.summary: When the world breaks apart into the void and End islands, the music now crossfades to the End's theme to match the End sky overhead — the normal soundtrack fades out as you approach the void, the End theme plays across the void and floating islands, and it fades back as the world reforms on the far side.
highlights: End music now plays across the void/End disintegration band
Smooth crossfade in and out, tied to your position so it matches the End-sky fade

entries.title: Smoother world-disintegration band
entries.summary: Crossing the band where the world breaks into the void and reforms as the End now generates much more smoothly. The empty void stretches are no longer built as full terrain and then immediately erased, and the floating End islands generate far faster — so there's noticeably less chunk-loading hitch as you travel through the band.
highlights: Void stretches no longer build terrain just to delete it
Floating End islands generate much faster
Less stutter crossing the disintegration band

entries.title: Leaner void/End band generation
entries.summary: A follow-up to the smoother disintegration band: the empty void and floating-End stretches no longer run the full overworld decoration and structure pass just to have it erased, so those chunks generate a little leaner still.
highlights: Void/End core chunks skip the wasted tree/ore/structure pass
Builds on the earlier disintegration-band generation speedup

entries.title: The train carves through the End islands
entries.summary: In the disintegration band — where the world dissolves into floating End islands — the train no longer skirts a cleared lane around the islands. It now bores tunnels straight through the islands in its path and rides out across the gaps on pillars, the same way it tunnels through hills and bridges ravines back in the overworld.
highlights: Tunnels are carved through End islands that block the track
Pillars rise from island tops; the track bridges the open void between islands

entries.title: End islands stay lit like the End
entries.summary: The floating End-stone islands that drift through the world's broken-apart stretches now stay lit even at night. Their surfaces no longer fall dark when the sun goes down, so those End zones keep their otherworldly glow around the clock to match the End sky already overhead.
highlights: End-stone islands no longer darken at night
Their glow fades in and out smoothly with the End sky as you cross each zone

entries.title: New advancements: the void journey and echoes of other players
entries.summary: Eight new advancements. Ride the train far enough and the world itself disintegrates — earn your way out to the void, across the floating End islands, and back to the overworld again. Plus a full set for echoes: meet, befriend, kill, or shove another player's echo (and your own) off the train into the void.
highlights: Voided Warranty, Tastes like Cheese?, Re-Over-World — reach the void, the End islands, then the overworld again
Repressed Memory — shove your own echo into the void
A Stranger's Ghost / Befriending the Dead / Not Your Ghost to Keep / Erased — encounter, befriend, kill, or void-shove another player's echo

entries.title: "Weakness, Then Gold" — a zombie's-cure story
entries.summary: A new book rides the train: seven letters by a zombie hunting its own cure — a Potion of Weakness from a witch, then a Golden Apple. The writing climbs from groans to human speech as the cure takes, and the last letter leaves the recipe for the next lost soul.
highlights: New 7-letter narrative book written from a zombie's point of view
Built on the real cure: a Potion of Weakness, then a Golden Apple

entries.title: End islands no longer missing whole chunks
entries.summary: Fixed the floating End-stone islands in the world-disintegration band sometimes dropping whole chunks — clean square gaps with full islands all around them. Islands now generate complete across the whole band.
highlights: Floating End islands in the disintegration band no longer have chunk-shaped holes punched out of them

entries.title: Soren: excited opener on Letter Two
entries.summary: Soren's second letter now opens with a burst of excitement as she realises the door is truly real.

entries.title: Trains stay put when you pause and resume
entries.summary: Pausing mid-ride (the singleplayer Esc menu) no longer strands you far behind with the train gone when you unpause. On resume you're set back on the train deck and it keeps rolling on.
highlights: Esc-pausing while riding — even for several minutes — keeps you aboard the train.
The train no longer drifts off and vanishes after a long pause.

entries.title: The train climbs real mountains into the Nether
entries.summary: A new world-gen band sends the train climbing through towering overworld mountains, tunnelling through the peaks, then crossing a real Nether core before easing back to the overworld. The mountains are full living terrain — grass, forests, flowers and snow-capped peaks grow on them, villages and other structures appear on their slopes, and the climb passes through highland biomes (groves, meadows, snowy slopes and jagged peaks). Bands that begin over ocean get a natural sand shore, and the Nether core glows with a warm, dim light.
highlights: Towering mountain ranges the train tunnels through on the way to the Nether
Mountains are real terrain: grass, trees, flowers, snow caps and structures generate on them
Altitude-graded highland biomes — forested base, spruce groves, snowy peaks
A real Nether core with warm dim lighting; ocean entrances get a sand shore

entries.title: The Nether core looks and feels like the real Nether
entries.summary: The real-Nether stretch the train crosses is now dressed with the game's own Nether world-gen instead of bare red rock: fire, glowstone, lava springs, mushrooms and mineable Nether ores (quartz, gold and ancient debris) generate through it, and it carries the Nether's red fog, ambient cave sounds and music.
highlights: Fire, glowstone, lava springs and mushrooms generate across the Nether core
Mine real Nether ores in the core — quartz, gold and ancient debris
The core now has the Nether's red fog, ambient sounds and music

entries.title: Nether-transition mountains ease into the world
entries.summary: The giant mountains that mark the Nether transition now grow out of the surrounding landscape gradually instead of rising as a sharp vertical wall, and their front weaves naturally across the terrain rather than running in a dead-straight line. Where the band meets the sea you now get real sandy beaches with soft dunes leading up into the slopes.
highlights: Mountains rise and fall smoothly from the natural terrain at both edges of the band — no more sudden cliff
The mountain front undulates across the landscape for a natural, non-straight edge
Ocean entrances form natural sand beaches that blend up into the hills

entries.title: Piglins keep their cool in the Nether band
entries.summary: Piglins, piglin brutes, and hoglins no longer shiver and convert when they're standing in the overworld's Nether biome. Inside the red-fog netherrack core of the Nether transition band they now behave exactly like they would in the real Nether — no zombification, no trembling. Step back out into the normal overworld and they go back to converting as usual.
highlights: Piglins & brutes no longer zombify in the Nether band's core
Hoglins no longer turn into zoglins there
No more trembling — behaviour reverts once they leave the Nether core

entries.title: Nether core now spans all five Nether biomes
entries.summary: The stretch of real Nether the train crosses no longer looks the same the whole way through. It now flows through all five Nether biomes the way the Nether itself generates them: crimson and warped forests with their fungi and nylium, soul sand valleys with soul fire and skeletons, basalt deltas with pillars and magma cubes, and the classic nether wastes. The ground, decoration, fog colour and mobs all change with the biome you're in.
highlights: All five Nether biomes generate across the Nether core
Per-biome ground, decoration, fog tint and mobs
Crimson forests bring piglins and hoglins (kept from zombifying in the band)

entries.title: Nether music across the transition band
entries.summary: The soundtrack now follows the world as your train crosses the Nether transition band: the overworld music fades away, and vanilla Nether music rises with the red glow of the netherrack core — then fades back to normal as you ride out the far side. It mirrors the End band, which already swaps to End music across the void.
highlights: Vanilla nether_wastes music now plays as you cross the netherrack core of the Nether transition band.
The track crossfades smoothly with your position — overworld music out on the way in, back in on the way out.

entries.title: The Nether core bridges lava on pillars
entries.summary: Crossing the real-Nether core, the train no longer rides through a solid netherrack tunnel the whole way. It now tunnels through the netherrack masses and rides out on open pillars across the lava lakes and caverns between them, just as it tunnels and bridges across the End islands. The tunnels and the train deck are kept clear of stray netherrack and stone, so the way ahead is always open.
highlights: The train bridges the Nether core's lava lakes on open pillars
Tunnels through netherrack masses, with the ride space kept clear of stray blocks

entries.title: Ghasts stop spawning trapped inside blocks in the Nether
entries.summary: Ghasts in the overworld Nether stretch sometimes spawned stuck inside the terrain and died instantly. They now only appear where there's real open air for their whole 4-block-wide body, so they float free and stick around to menace the train.
highlights: Ghasts no longer spawn embedded in blocks and die on the spot

entries.title: The Nether band's core now plays by Nether rules
entries.summary: The red-fog netherrack core of the Nether transition band now behaves like the real Nether, not just looks like it. Water you pour boils away in a hiss of steam, beds explode if you try to sleep in them, and wet sponges dry out the instant you place them. The sky over the core stays clear too — no rain, no clouds and no lightning, even in a thunderstorm. Step back out into the normal overworld and water, beds and weather all work exactly as usual again.

entries.title: The sky turns to the Nether's in the transition band
entries.summary: Crossing into the Nether band, the overworld sky now fades away — sun, moon, stars and blue gradient give way to the skyless red glow of the real Nether overhead, then fade back to a normal sky on the way out. The sky takes on whichever Nether biome you're passing through (crimson red, warped teal, soul-sand blue, basalt grey or wastes red), so the sky and the fog are one.
highlights: Sun, moon, stars and blue sky fade out as you enter the Nether core
The sky's colour matches the Nether biome you're in — and the fog
Smoothly crossfades in and out, with no pop at the band edges

entries.title: Train stays dry — external water & lava kept out
entries.summary: Water and lava from the outside world no longer flow into or through the train. External fluid is stopped at the carriage edge and cleared from inside the train's footprint each tick, so decks and interiors stay dry even as the train crosses rivers, lakes and oceans.
highlights: External water & lava no longer flood the train's deck or interior
The train stays dry while crossing water

entries.title: Nether train tunnel stays clean and dry
entries.summary: The train tunnel through the Nether no longer fills with stray basalt, the Nether stretch is now dry like the real Nether, and gravel can no longer tumble onto the rails as you ride through it.
highlights: Basalt and other Nether decoration no longer intrude into the train tunnel
The Nether stretch is dry — worldgen water in the netherrack mountains is cleared
Gravel no longer falls onto the tracks in the Nether

entries.title: New Quiet Rule: mend worn gear by combining it
entries.summary: The Quiet Rules of the Train book can now turn up a new rule hinting that worn-down weapons and armour can be mended by combining two of the same item — and that the result keeps the stats of whichever piece you place on the left.

entries.title: Temporarily roll back the pause/resume train fix
entries.summary: We've rolled back the recent change that kept you aboard the train across a singleplayer pause, while we work on a better fix for a new issue it introduced. As a stop-gap the older behaviour returns: pausing mid-ride can again leave you behind with the train having rolled on ahead. A proper fix is in progress.
highlights: Reverts the v0.354.0 pause/resume rider fix as a temporary stop-gap.
Known trade-off: pausing while riding can again strand you behind the moving train until the proper fix lands.

entries.title: Beds only explode in the Nether (and a clearer respawn message)
entries.summary: Beds no longer blow up when you sleep in the normal overworld — that only happens in the real Nether now, like vanilla. And sleeping in a bed gives a clearer message that your respawn point rides along with the train.
highlights: Beds are safe to use everywhere except the actual Nether core
Sleeping shows a message making clear your respawn moves with the train

entries.title: New advancement: Hell on Rails
entries.summary: Ride the train deep into the Nether stretch and earn a new advancement, Hell on Rails. It unlocks once you're well inside the netherrack — not the moment you cross the edge — and now sits just before Voided Warranty on the advancement tree.
highlights: New "Hell on Rails" advancement for reaching the Nether band aboard the train
Placed before Voided Warranty in the advancement tree, matching the journey order

entries.title: Train villagers no longer share the same name and trades
entries.summary: Villagers riding the train sometimes spawned as identical twins — the same name and the same trades in every carriage of a given type. Each train villager now gets its own randomly generated name and a freshly rolled set of trades, while still keeping the carriage's profession (an armorer carriage still has an armorer).
highlights: Each train villager gets a unique name
Trades are rolled fresh per villager instead of repeating
Shop professions (armorer, toolsmith, leatherworker, …) are preserved

entries.title: Longer Nether crossing — real-Nether core now spans 5,000 blocks
entries.summary: The real-Nether leg of the journey now runs 5,000 blocks — the same length as the End-island stretch — so the train spends a substantial stretch crossing genuine Nether terrain (red sky, netherrack, nether mobs) instead of a brief flicker before the world reassembles into the overworld.
highlights: Real-Nether core span increased from 400 to 5,000 blocks
Now as long as the End-island stretch of the loop

entries.title: New carriages: shops, library rooms, maze rooms, and shrines
entries.summary: A fresh batch of carriages now rides the train. Two new villager shops (an archer stall and a cleric), three new maze rooms, two new library cars, and four landmark feature carriages — a statue, a grave, a worship shrine, and a wither room — all appear as variants of their existing carriage types.
highlights: New shop carriages: archer and cleric
New maze rooms: two layouts plus a doors room
New library carriages: a two-lane library and a book-door room
New feature carriages: statue, grave, worship shrine, and wither room

entries.title: Item stats grow with difficulty
entries.summary: Weapons and armour found deeper into the run now hit harder and hold up better. Each difficulty level adds a flat bonus to an item's main stat — attack damage for weapons, armour for armour — with axes gaining a little extra.
