Applies to chest, container and vase loot, and to the gear carried by player-like mobs.
highlights: +0.1 attack damage / armour per difficulty level (+0.15 for axes)
Scales chest, container and vase loot, plus PlayerMob equipment
Regular mob gear and the secondary stats (attack speed, toughness) are unchanged

entries.title: No more waterlogged stairs in the Nether tunnel
entries.summary: The train tunnel and its pillar staircases no longer come out waterlogged when the track crosses the Nether band. The Nether stays bone-dry — no stray water seeping into the stone-brick stairs, slabs, or walls of the corridor.
highlights: Tunnel & pillar stairs stay dry through the Nether band

entries.title: New carriage variants
entries.summary: Five new carriages join the train: two additional statue carriages, a second worship carriage, and two new shop carriages (a smithing variant and a leatherworker variant). Also added a wither barrel loot table.

entries.title: No more basalt on the Nether train tracks
entries.summary: Crossing the Nether band, basalt from the basalt-delta stretches could still end up on the train tracks — sitting on the rails, the bed, or the centre line — and it was most stubborn right at the leading edge, appearing on track that had just generated ahead of you. The corridor cleanup now catches that late-arriving basalt and re-lays the real track over anything it buried, so the tunnel stays clear the whole way through.
highlights: Stray basalt no longer ends up on the rails or bed as you ride through the Nether band

entries.title: Witch Carriage
entries.summary: A new enemy carriage haunted by a witch joins the train roster. The huskskel carriage also received improved loot chests.
highlights: New witch carriage with a witch mob
Huskskel chests now use randomised loot pools (weapons, armour, food)

entries.title: Remote echo encounter stories now always post to Discord
entries.summary: A passenger's Discord story was silently skipped when a remote echo (a traveller from another world's past life) spawned on a distant carriage and the player walked up later. The encounter journal now opens retroactively on first close approach, so every remote echo meeting — however it starts — gets its story.

entries.title: Live mirroring in the template editor (X / Y / Z, all templates)
entries.summary: The in-game template editor now mirrors your edits live as you build: place or break a block and its reflections update instantly across the enabled axes — no save needed. A new vertical Y axis joins the existing left/right (X) and front/back (Z) mirrors, flipping slabs, stairs, doors and trapdoors the right way up. Mirroring is now available on every editor template (carriages, parts, tracks, pillars, tunnels), off by default; tunnels keep their existing horizontal mirroring. Toggle Mirror X / Y / Z from the editor's X-menu. Generated structures are unchanged.
highlights: Edits mirror live across the enabled axes while you build
New Y (vertical) axis — slabs/stairs/doors flip top-to-bottom
Mirror toggles on every editor template, off by default (tunnels keep X+Z on)
Single Mirror X / Y / Z row in the editor X-menu

entries.title: Nether track & tunnel set + level/phase spawn rules for train templates
entries.summary: The Nether stretch of the train now has its own look: blackstone track tiles, tunnel sections, and tunnel portals take over from the overworld stone-brick set as you ride through it. Under the hood, any weighted train template — carriages, interiors, track, and tunnels — can now be limited to a difficulty-level range and to specific world phases (Overworld, Nether, Void, End), and authors set those limits right in the in-game editor.
highlights: New blackstone-themed Nether track tiles, tunnel sections, and tunnel portals that appear only in the Nether band
Train templates can be gated to a min/max difficulty level and to chosen world phases (Overworld / Nether / Void / End)
In-editor min/max-level steppers and a phase selector on every weighted template — on both the menu and the floating world-space panels

entries.title: Dark Nether carriage parts
entries.summary: New dark carriage part set built from polished blackstone, nether bricks, and basalt. The fire template now uses these parts exclusively and only spawns in the Nether.
highlights: Dark walls, floor, roof, and doors with Nether-block palette
Fire carriage now Nether-phase-only

entries.title: Carriages reskin by dimension — Nether-themed dark carriages
entries.summary: Carriage parts (floors, walls, roofs, doors) can now be restricted to a Diff-Level range and to specific dimensions, so the train's carriages visibly change as it crosses the world. A new Nether-themed dark carriage set (blackstone & nether brick) rides through the Nether band, and the change carries the same in-world editor controls used for the rest of the train: per-part minimum/maximum Diff-Level and Overworld/Nether/Void/End toggles (shift-click a dimension to solo it). Nether carriage theming now begins at the same point the Nether track does.
highlights: Carriage parts can be gated by Diff-Level range and by dimension (Overworld/Nether/Void/End)
New Nether-themed dark carriage set appears in the Nether band
Editor: ≥min / ≤max / O N V E columns per part; shift-click a dimension toggles all-but-that-one (shared across every dimension menu)
Fix: Nether carriage parts now begin where the Nether track does (previously lagged ~1000 blocks)

entries.title: Smoother Nether-band travel — no more lag spikes
entries.summary: Riding through the Nether section of the world no longer causes multi-second freezes. The system that keeps stray Nether blocks (basalt and friends) off the train tracks was rewritten to run far more efficiently, so the rails stay clear without the game hitching as new terrain generates ahead of you.
highlights: Fixed the multi-second server freezes when traveling into freshly-generated Nether-band terrain.
Track-clearing now keeps pace even when many chunks generate at once.

entries.title: Tunnels & tracks fade into the Nether block by block
entries.summary: Crossing into the Nether transition, the train's stone-brick tunnels and tracks now dissolve into their dark Nether forms one block at a time — a grainy crossfade that thickens toward the core, in step with the ground turning to netherrack — instead of every section switching over all at once. The whole transition is also stretched out much longer for a more gradual descent into the Nether.
highlights: Tunnels and tracks crossfade block-by-block between their Overworld and Nether-dark looks
Dark blocks creep in as a grainy dither that thickens toward the Nether core
The Nether transition is now far longer and more gradual

entries.title: Leaner world generation
entries.summary: World generation now reuses a single cached band-layout calculation instead of rebuilding it for every terrain column — previously it was recomputed millions of times per session. This cuts redundant work and memory churn while terrain generates (including around the Nether and End transition bands), with no change to the world that's produced.
highlights: Same world, generated more efficiently — no change to terrain, structures, or the train.

entries.title: Editor Menus toggle hides all world-space editor panels
entries.summary: The in-game editor's X-menu now has a single 'Editor Menus' toggle that hides every floating world-space panel at once — the help board, the carriage/contents/track lists, the part and block-variant menus, and plot labels — for an unobstructed view while building. The X menu stays available so you can flip everything back on anytime.
highlights: One toggle hides or shows all editor world-space menus
Any open part, block-variant, and container menus close when toggled off

entries.title: Echo encounter stories describe the echo's gear and your words
entries.summary: When you meet a remote echo aboard the train, its Discord story now paints a fuller picture: it names the echo's two finest items — enchantments, armour trim and all — notes any better gear it claims along the way, and remembers if you spoke to it. Your words are sometimes quoted in the tale, sometimes just hinted at, and are always kept private if you'd recently been in touch with the dev.
highlights: The echo's two best items (with enchantments & armour trim) now appear in its story
Better gear the echo picks up mid-encounter is added as it happens
Chatting near an echo can become part of the story — your words quoted or kept private

entries.title: Editor mirror now reflects block variants
entries.summary: The template editor's XYZ mirror gains an opt-in "V" toggle that also mirrors per-cell block-variant pools across the enabled axes — so you can author one half of a carriage or part and get the symmetric variants for free. Off by default, so existing templates are unchanged.
highlights: New V checkbox beside the X / Y / Z mirror toggles in the editor menu
Variant pools mirror live as you add, paste, or break — with a save-time backstop
Block facing, slab/stair half, and lock-group ids all reflect correctly

entries.title: Editor Stages — reusable difficulty/dimension presets
entries.summary: Content creators using the in-game editor can now define named Stages — reusable presets of the difficulty-level and dimension gating that decides where a template is allowed to spawn — and apply them to carriages, contents, parts, and tracks. Edit a Stage once and every template linked to it retunes together. This release also fixes Nether-gated content so it appears across the whole Nether stretch instead of only deep in its core.
highlights: A global Stages list sits beside the editor's template menu, with inline add / remove / edit of each preset's level band and dimensions.
Pick a Stage (or 'Custom') from any gate-editing spot — including per carriage-part entry — and linked templates update live when you edit the Stage.
Fix: Nether-gated templates and Stages now trigger as the Nether terrain begins, not hundreds of blocks into the Nether core.

entries.title: Train carriages no longer lost when you pause and resume
entries.summary: Pausing a singleplayer game on a moving train — via the Esc menu, reading a book, or an advancement popup — could make the carriages around you disappear and stay gone until you reloaded the world. The train is now held in place across the pause so its carriages survive it.
highlights: The whole train is kept loaded through the pause, then eases back to normal once you've settled back on board.

entries.title: Launcher info in the world-info Discord post
entries.summary: The one-time world-info message posted to Discord when you start a new world now reports which launcher you're running under — Modrinth, CurseForge, the vanilla launcher, or a dedicated server — alongside the launcher version when it's available. Makes it easier to reproduce and debug a run from its world-info post.
highlights: World-info Discord post now shows your launcher (Modrinth / CurseForge / vanilla / dedicated server)
Includes the launcher version when the launcher reports it

entries.title: Stages ship as default content + auto-promote in dev
entries.summary: The named gate Stages you build in the editor (difficulty-level + dimension presets) now ship with the mod itself. The default carriages come pre-tuned with shipped Stages, and the standard carriage gains new sandstone parts that appear in a desert stretch. For content creators, Stage edits made in dev mode now commit to the source tree and ship in the next build, the same way the rest of the editor content does.
highlights: Three Stages (desert / nether / stone) and sandstone carriage parts now ship as defaults; the default carriages use them to gate which parts appear where.
Editor Stage edits auto-promote to source in dev mode, so authored presets ship with the next build.
Fix: part-to-Stage links on the default carriages now resolve (they previously fell back to a plain gate because the Stages weren't bundled in the jar).

entries.title: Editor part menus stay open by look direction
entries.summary: In the carriage-template editor, the FLOOR/WALLS/ROOF/DOORS part menus used to vanish the moment your crosshair left a solid block — looking at an open doorway, a part set to none, the hollow interior, or the roof gap closed them. They now stay visible as long as you're inside the carriage plot and looking toward a part, so you can edit a part even while facing its empty space.
highlights: Part menus track look direction instead of requiring a solid block under the crosshair
Editing a wall/door side while facing its open air now targets the side you're looking at

entries.title: New sandstone carriage parts
entries.summary: Fresh sandstone carriage parts join the rotation — windowed, broken, and open-lattice sandstone walls plus an open sandstone roof — woven into several existing carriages for more variety on the train.
highlights: Windowed, broken, and open sandstone wall variants
New open sandstone roof part

entries.title: /feedback allowed in Free Play; /kill restricted to self only
entries.summary: Using /feedback (the bug-report command) no longer flags your run as Free Play. /kill with a target selector (e.g. /kill @e) now correctly taints the run — only bare /kill (self) remains clean.

entries.title: Duplicating a template keeps its block-variant grouping
entries.summary: When you create a new part, carriage, or contents template from 'Current', the editor now also copies the block-variant grouping (locked cells that roll the same variant together), not just the per-cell variant options. Duplicated locked groups stay grouped instead of having to be re-linked by hand.
highlights: New-from-current now carries over variant lock groups, not just variant options
Applies to part, carriage, and contents duplicates

entries.title: Carriages switch theme in step with the world around them
entries.summary: Train carriages now change their look to match the dimension they're passing through right at the border, instead of lagging far behind it. Crossing into the End from the void and back into the Overworld from the Nether now re-theme the train promptly.
highlights: No more stretches of out-of-place carriages deep into the End or after leaving the Nether

entries.title: Copper carriages, new floors, and a copper stage
entries.summary: A new copper theme runs through the train: oxidized-copper walls (solid, windowed, open, and rusted), matching copper roofs (solid, open, and windowed) and a copper door now appear on carriages. Two new floors join the mix — obsidian and tuff — and a dedicated copper stage governs where these coppery carriages show up along the run, with the copper, desert, and stone level bands retuned around it.
highlights: New copper-themed walls, roofs, and door parts across multiple carriage templates
New obsidian and tuff floor parts
New copper stage with retuned copper/desert/stone level bands

entries.title: New enemy carriages: Evoker, Breeze, and Elder Guardian
entries.summary: Three new enemy carriage types have been added to the train — Evoker, Breeze, and Elder Guardian — each with their own loot. The stone stage now scales up to level 10.
highlights: Evoker carriage with totem and rare loot
Breeze carriage with wind charge drops
Elder Guardian carriage with mining fatigue threat
Stone stage extended to max level 10

entries.title: Editor: stage & spawn-gating for content sub-variants
entries.summary: The in-game content editor now lets you gate individual sub-variants of a content group, not just top-level variants. Each member of a group (e.g. the mobs inside an "enemies" group) can be linked to a Stage or given its own spawn band (Diff-Level range + dimensions), straight from the Sub-Variants panel — so you can make one sub-variant appear only in the Nether while another stays in the Overworld.
highlights: Sub-variant rows now show the same Stage chip and level/dimension cells as top-level content variants; the "(default)" row stays weight-only.
Gated sub-variants are filtered at spawn time; if a band excludes every member, the group parent's own contents fill the slot instead.

entries.title: Report bugs from the death screen
entries.summary: The after-death feedback survey now asks whether you hit any bugs this run. Pick what happened — Train Vanished, Lag, or Other (or No) — and add as much detail as you like. If you report a bug, your most recent game logs are bundled up and sent along so the creator can finally see what went wrong on your machine.
highlights: New "Did you face any bugs in this run?" feedback question
Reporting a bug attaches your recent logs so the creator can diagnose issues they can't reproduce

entries.title: Report a bug straight from the death screen
entries.summary: When a run ends, a new red "Submit Bug" button on the death screen takes you straight to the bug-report question — no paging through every feedback prompt — and your recent logs ride along to help diagnose it. Once you've answered, the button turns green to confirm the report was sent, and any feedback question you've already answered now shows a "Sent" badge.
highlights: New "Submit Bug" shortcut on the death screen jumps straight to the bug report, under a "Did you hit a bug?" prompt.
Feedback questions now show a green "Sent" badge once your answer has been submitted.

entries.title: Echo item stat rounding
entries.summary: Echo item stats now round to one decimal place (e.g. 7.5 attack), dropping the trailing .0 for whole numbers

entries.title: Lag bug reports now include your system specs
entries.summary: When you report a "Lag" bug from the death-screen feedback survey, the report now includes a quick snapshot of your setup — the memory your launcher gave the game, plus CPU, GPU, RAM, OS and current FPS — so performance problems are far easier to diagnose. Other bug types are unchanged.
highlights: Reporting "Lag" now attaches your system specs (CPU, GPU, RAM, OS, FPS)
Includes the memory CurseForge/Modrinth allocated to the game (max heap / -Xmx)
Specs are collected only for lag reports; other bug answers are unaffected

entries.title: Repeat bug reports no longer overwrite earlier logs
entries.summary: When you send a bug report after dying, your game logs are archived on the server. Sending more than one report from the same game version used to overwrite the previous report's logs — each submission now lands in its own timestamped folder, so nothing is lost.
highlights: Each bug-report submission is archived to its own timestamped subfolder

entries.title: Feedback survey responses collected in a dedicated channel
entries.summary: Feedback-survey answers now also post to a dedicated Discord survey-results channel (in addition to each player's thread), with a link back to the original, so the team can review feedback in one place.
highlights: Survey answers mirrored to a dedicated channel with a jump-link to the thread

entries.title: Train no longer vanishes when the world saves
entries.summary: Fixed the train disappearing (and regenerating from scratch) when the world autosaved. The whole train is now held in place across every save, so riding through an autosave — or running /save-all — keeps every carriage and its contents intact.
highlights: Train survives autosaves and manual saves
No more lost or regenerated carriages on save

entries.title: Maze room variety rebalance
entries.summary: Tuned how often the maze carriage's room layouts appear — the 2nd-level, hall, two-way, sides, and up variants now roll more often relative to the rest.

entries.title: Carriages no longer vanish at low simulation distance
entries.summary: Fixed the train permanently losing carriages — appearing to vanish or regenerate — when playing at a low Simulation Distance. Carriages generated ahead of the train are now kept loaded until they've been saved, so they reliably come back instead of disappearing for good.
highlights: Carriages no longer vanish permanently when riding with a low simulation distance
Newly generated carriages ahead of the train stay loaded until saved, then reload reliably as you approach

entries.title: Quieter logs while riding long trains
entries.summary: Long trains no longer flood the server log with a harmless, repeated Sable holding-handshake message during travel, and the matching redundant per-tick work is trimmed. No gameplay change.

entries.title: Locked to the tested Sable version
entries.summary: Dungeon Train now requires the exact version of Sable it was built and tested against. If a different Sable is installed, the game stops at startup with a clear message naming the version it needs, instead of loading against an untested physics build (which could crash or break the train). The modpacks already include the correct Sable, so most players won't notice a change.
highlights: Refuses to start against a mismatched Sable version, with a clear 'requires Sable' message
Protects against crashes and broken trains from an untested Sable build

entries.title: Talk with the dev from the main menu
entries.summary: You can now talk directly with the dev without leaving the game. When the dev sends you a message, an envelope appears on the title screen — unread messages pop up a preview and make the envelope pulse. Open it for a simple conversation window: the dev's replies arrive live while you're there, and you can write back any time. Messages you send while offline are delivered automatically when you reconnect, and your feedback answers appear in the conversation too.
highlights: Talk directly with the dev, right from the main menu
An envelope appears on the title screen when the dev has messaged you
Unread messages show a preview popup and a pulsing envelope
Replies arrive live while the chat is open, and you can write back
Messages sent while offline are delivered when you reconnect

entries.title: Smoother rides on long trains — carriages no longer stutter or teleport
entries.summary: Fixed carriages jittering or jumping across large distances while you ride a long, moving train, and cleared the physics-desync errors that came with it. Carriages near you now stay loaded smoothly as the train slides along, so the ride holds together even far from where the train started.
highlights: Carriages near you stay put instead of snapping to a new spot after the game briefly unloaded them
Long trains no longer thrash between loaded and unloaded as you travel, keeping the whole train steady around you

entries.title: Basic Vivecraft (VR) support on the train
entries.summary: Play with the Vivecraft VR mod? Two things are better on the train now. Your melee swings can finally find mobs on a moving carriage — the hit-detection query used to abort on Sable's moving sub-levels and hit nothing. And a VR-only log-spam that hitched your frame rate while riding is fixed. No effect unless you install Vivecraft yourself — it's not part of the modpack.
highlights: VR (Vivecraft) melee can now target mobs while you're on a moving carriage
Fixed VR-only frame hitching caused by log spam on the train
Vivecraft-only — nothing changes unless you install it (it's not in the modpack)

entries.title: Quieter world-load — no more Sable sub-level log spam
entries.summary: Freshly-loaded trains now settle for a brief moment before departing, so entering a world no longer prints a one-time burst of harmless "non-existent sub-level" errors to the log. The train runs identically once it's moving — this is a startup and logging cleanup, not a gameplay change.
highlights: World-load no longer logs a burst of harmless Sable sub-level errors
Trains ease into motion from a brief standstill when a world loads

entries.title: Quartz build set and new dungeon content
entries.summary: A new quartz-themed build set (walls, floor, roof, doors) joins the dungeon templates, and new copper, shulker, and evoker enemy content has been added alongside tweaks to elder and maze encounters.
highlights: New quartz walls, floor, roof, and door parts
New copper, shulker, and evoker2 content prefabs
Quartz stage level range extended (40-50 -> 40-60)

entries.title: Maze and zombie-room prefab tweaks
entries.summary: Rebalanced the maze dungeon's copper stage odds and refreshed variant data for the zombie-room and corridor prefabs.
highlights: Copper stage now appears more often in the maze dungeon
Zombie-room encounters rebalanced (command block/mob spawn odds)

entries.title: Consent prompt for Developer messages
entries.summary: If the developer sends you a message while you're in a world, you'll see a prompt asking if you want to receive it — type @Dev to accept. Once you accept, further messages from the developer show up in your chat automatically for the rest of your session (or for 20 minutes after you last replied). If you don't accept, the message is never shown in your in-game chat, but you can still find it in the main-menu chat panel.
highlights: New: an opt-in prompt before Developer messages appear in your in-game chat
Accepting once keeps future messages flowing for the session (or ~20 minutes of activity)

entries.title: New /dungeontrain difficulty command
entries.summary: Server operators can now set the run's difficulty on the fly with /dungeontrain difficulty <level>, instead of waiting to earn it by travelling. The whole game shifts to match at once — the difficulty shown on the HUD, the mobs and their gear, the carriage stages that generate ahead, the loot, and villager trades. It re-anchors each time you run it and drifts with real travel afterward; /dungeontrain difficulty auto clears it and returns to normal progression.
highlights: Set any difficulty level instantly for testing or custom runs
Shifts the HUD, mobs, carriage stages, loot and trades together
/dungeontrain difficulty auto restores automatic, travel-based scaling

entries.title: Template Blocks menu — reskin a build in one pass
entries.summary: A new in-editor Template Blocks menu lets builders swap a block everywhere in a build at once (walls, floors, variants and all) with whatever they're holding, while keeping doors, stairs, and slabs facing the right way. Also added a new mud building set — doors, floors, roofs, and walls — for browner, earthier builds.
highlights: New Template Blocks menu (press V, or /dungeontrain editor blocks) — swap a block everywhere in a build in one click
New mud building set: doors, floors, roofs, and walls

entries.title: Echo appearances counted in the community data
entries.summary: When a fallen player's echo returns in another world, the mod now reports it to the community data dashboard so we can see how often each death is remembered. No change to how echoes look or behave in-game.

entries.title: New maze stages (Stone, Desert, Quartz) + mushroom rooms
entries.summary: The maze dungeon now has Stone, Desert, and Quartz stages alongside the existing Copper stage, so its theme changes as you go deeper into a run. Two new mushroom-themed rooms can also show up — a mushroom feature room, and a fuller mushroom grove later on. Bookshelf and pot arrangements in library rooms also look a bit more consistent.
highlights: New Stone, Desert, and Quartz maze stages (joins the existing Copper stage)
New mushroom feature room and mushroom grove room
Minor visual polish to bookshelf/pot layouts in library rooms

entries.title: New worlds always start in the Overworld
entries.summary: New Dungeon Train worlds can now only be created with an Overworld start — the Nether and End starting-dimension options have been removed from the Create World screen. Worlds that already started in the Nether or End are unaffected and keep working exactly as before.
highlights: Removed the Nether/End starting-dimension world types from world creation
Existing Nether/End-start worlds are unaffected and continue to work

entries.title: Editor tooling no longer runs during normal play
entries.summary: The in-game editor's plot-detection was running on every server tick for every player — even during normal play with the editor closed, and on dedicated servers where nobody is editing. It now only runs when you're up at the editor build area, so regular gameplay no longer pays for that editor overhead. Most noticeable on longer, content-heavy trains and on dedicated servers.
highlights: Editor overlay skipped during normal play (all game modes)
Recovers up to ~9ms of server time per tick on a long train
Applies to singleplayer and dedicated servers

entries.title: Death-screen New World/Same World always start in the Overworld
entries.summary: The death screen's "New World" and "Same World" buttons now always start your next run in the Overworld, instead of an occasional random chance of starting fresh in the Nether or the End. The vanilla in-place "Respawn" button is unaffected and keeps its existing chance of relocating you to a different dimension.
highlights: Death-screen New World/Same World starts are always Overworld now
Vanilla Respawn's dimension-shift chance is unchanged

entries.title: Smoother train growth
entries.summary: New carriages no longer cause a server hitch as the train extends. The terrain a spawning carriage needs is now prepared in the background ahead of time instead of stalling the tick on the spot — so riding fast or flying in creative no longer stutters (or trips "Can't keep up!" warnings) each time the train grows.
highlights: Carriage block placement is also much cheaper on the server tick now.

entries.title: Smoother performance on long trains
entries.summary: Very long trains no longer bog down the server tick. Once a train grows large, its physics switches to a lighter step rate that roughly halves the heaviest part of the per-tick cost, so TPS holds up on big trains and busy multiplayer servers. Short and medium trains are unaffected and keep full-fidelity collision.
highlights: Large trains shed ~25-45% of their physics tick cost
Engages only when a train is big enough to matter — short trains keep full-fidelity physics

entries.title: Developer messages now stay in your thread
entries.summary: A reply from the developer now reaches only the player whose Discord thread it was posted in — previously every player who had accepted developer messages saw it. Messages the developer posts outside a player's thread no longer appear in-game.

entries.title: Suspicious stew from loot now has an effect
entries.summary: Suspicious stew found in chests, decorated pots, and other loot containers previously did nothing when eaten. It now grants a real effect, just like a suspicious stew you'd craft yourself.
highlights: Container-found suspicious stew now applies a potion effect when eaten
Effect is randomly chosen from the same set a crafted suspicious stew can have
Thanks to generalefficiency's Twitch stream for catching this one!

entries.title: Pacifist advancement chain (100/250/1000 carriages)
entries.summary: Three new advancements reward a peaceful ride — reach 100, then 250, then 1,000 carriages in a single life without dealing any damage to anything. Thanks to generalefficiency's Twitch stream for the idea!
highlights: Pacifist — 100 carriages, zero damage dealt
Turn the Other Cheek — 250 carriages, zero damage dealt
Peace Train — 1,000 carriages, zero damage dealt

entries.title: New Nether enemies + mud brick oak decor parts
entries.summary: Three new enemies join the fight: wither skeletons and blazes now stalk Nether-phase carriages, and a rebalanced bogged variant adds more skeletal archer variety throughout the train. A new mud-brick oak part set (doors, floors, roofs, walls) is now available across all carriage templates for richer room decoration.
highlights: New wither skeleton enemy (Nether-phase carriages)
New blaze enemy (Nether-phase carriages)
New bogged variant with rebalanced spawn weights
New mud brick oak part set (doors/floor/roof/walls) across all templates

entries.title: Editor Menus toggle now works in every template
entries.summary: The X menu's Editor Menus toggle (hides the in-world editor help board, type list, and plot labels) previously only showed up while editing a carriage. It now appears whenever you're editing any template — carriages, contents, tracks, architecture, or individual parts.

entries.title: Random books no longer reshuffle their story on reselect
entries.summary: Random books found in chests could quietly swap to a different title and story every time you scrolled past them in your hotbar. They now lock in their story the first time you pick one up.

entries.title: Desert seating variants + new zombie rooms
entries.summary: Desert-stage carriages gained new sand-themed library and seating decor, and the enemies pool grew three new zombie-room encounters.
highlights: New sandlib desert-stage library decor
New sand_seat_thin desert seating variant
Three new zombie room encounters: zombieroom2, antizombiesroom, zombiejail

entries.title: Quartz & obsidian carriage parts + a desert temple
entries.summary: New quartz and obsidian building parts now dress the train's carriages in the mid-progression bands, and a desert temple room can appear among the train's contents.
highlights: New quartz & obsidian floors, walls, roofs and a door, wired across several carriage templates
New desert temple content room

entries.title: Editor difficulty bands go up to level 1000
entries.summary: In the in-game editor, Stage, template, and mob difficulty-band levels can now be set up to 1000 (previously capped at 100), so content can be gated for very high difficulty tiers.

entries.title: Community shared books: write, burn, and discover
entries.summary: Sign a book & quill and it burns away, carried off to become part of a shared, world-spanning library — its text (screened for inappropriate content) can later be found by other players. As you read the train's own hand-written books, community books start turning up in dungeon chests, each credited to its author.
highlights: Signing a book & quill sends it out into the world instead of keeping it — it burns away
Discover books written by other players in dungeon chest loot, credited to their author
Community books appear more often the more of the game's own random books you've read (up to ~75%)
All shared writing is moderated before anyone can find it

entries.title: Advancements for signing and reading shared books
entries.summary: Signing a book & quill to send into the community pool now earns the 'Ghostwriter' advancement, and finding a real player's book in a dungeon chest earns 'A Stranger's Story'. Also fixes a bug where community books could stop appearing in chests entirely after a server had been running for a little while.
highlights: New advancement: Ghostwriter (sign a book to share with the community)
New advancement: A Stranger's Story (find and read another player's shared book)
Fixed: community books could stop dropping in chests after the first ~30 seconds

entries.title: Advancement rework: Nether Return Again, End of the Line, Inter-Reality Passenger
entries.summary: Nether Return Again now rewards looping all the way around the disintegration band and back into the End, instead of reading every Nether starting book. The End-islands advancement (formerly 'Tastes like Cheese?') is renamed to End of the Line, replacing the old book-reading advancement of that name. Inter-Reality Passenger is temporarily disabled pending new Overworld-reality content.
highlights: Nether Return Again: earn it by completing a full loop (void, End islands, back to solid overworld ground) and then riding back into the End a second time
'Tastes like Cheese?' renamed to 'End of the Line'; the old book-reading advancement with that title has been removed
Re-Over-World and Nether Return Again are now hidden in the advancement tree until earned
Inter-Reality Passenger is temporarily disabled

entries.title: Richer End-band biomes + hidden endgame milestones
entries.summary: The train's End band now generates the full variety of End biomes as you cross it, instead of a single repeated one. The deepest exploration achievements have also been reworked and tucked away as hidden surprises to discover on your travels.
highlights: All of the End's biomes now appear across the End band
Late-game dimension milestones are now hidden until you reach them

entries.title: New dungeon content: pots, sculk, mossmaze, sponge, ravanger, worf, quartz seating
entries.summary: A batch of new decor and encounter variety: pots and sculk-themed dirt rooms, a sponge-covered moss variant, a mossy maze room, a ravanger enemy for deep runs, a worf cow variant, and quartz seating for the obsidian/quartz stages.
highlights: New pots and sculk dirt-room variants
Sponge reskin of the moss room
New mossmaze room
Ravanger enemy (deep-level encounters)
Worf cow variant
Quartz seating decor for obsidian/quartz stages

entries.title: Carriage interiors are properly lit again
entries.summary: Fixed train carriage interiors rendering too dark — floors, walls, and light sources like torches and lanterns inside carriages now light up correctly as you ride through the train.

entries.title: Rainbow flag, desert maze room, and campfire writing loot
entries.summary: Rainbow banner displays can now appear as a decorative feature. Added a new desert maze room, a books-and-writing-supplies loot barrel to campfire spots, and rebalanced a batch of enemy spawn difficulty and maze weights.
highlights: Rainbow flag banner display can now spawn as a decorative feature
New desert maze room segment
Campfire spots can now hold a writing-supplies loot barrel (books, ink, paper)
Rebalanced enemy minLevel gating and slime spawn difficulty

entries.title: Community books ease into a fair share of chest loot
entries.summary: Player-written community books found in dungeon chests now taper off as your world reads more of them. Instead of staying loot-heavy once you've discovered them, they settle toward an even share alongside the built-in book pool, so late-run chest loot feels more varied.
highlights: Community (player-written) books taper toward an even share of chest loot the more of them a world reads

entries.title: Contents editor nests sub-variants under their group
entries.summary: In the in-game Contents template picker, carriage-content sub-variants (e.g. the Desert/Copper/Quartz/Stone members of Maze) no longer appear as top-level rows beside their parent. Selecting a group parent now opens a sub-list of its members plus an entry for the parent's own contents, matching the underlying data hierarchy.
highlights: Group members no longer clutter the top-level Contents list
Group parents drill into a sub-variant sub-menu

entries.title: Desert vault: reduced hidden-feature frequency
entries.summary: The desert vault's hidden decorative feature now appears less often in a few of its spawn slots.

entries.title: Fixed a world-generation freeze when riding forward
entries.summary: Riding the train forward over slow-to-generate terrain could freeze world generation and eventually hang the server. Trains now make sure the ground ahead has finished generating before extending, so the ride stays smooth.
highlights: No more world-gen freeze/hang when the train outruns terrain generation

entries.title: Advancements reveal as you play, plus three new ones
entries.summary: The Dungeon Train advancement tab is no longer a wall of spoilers — advancements now stay hidden until you reach the one before them, revealing the next steps as you progress. Three new advancements join the tree, and a few older ones were tidied up.
highlights: Progressive 'fog-of-war' reveal: each advancement unhides once its parent is earned
New: 'Others?' — come within 4 blocks of another passenger
New: 'The Enchiridion' — read (and close) your first book of any kind
New: 'Summon The Creator' — tag @dev or @brennanhatton in chat
'Everything Burrito' now branches straight off the Dungeon Train root
Renamed 'Hell on Rails' to 'Inter-Dimensional Passenger'; removed two redundant boarding advancements

entries.title: Books-written tally on the death screen
entries.summary: The death screen now counts how many books you wrote and sent off to the train this life. A book & quill icon sits beside your books-read count on the gear page — hover it to see "Books written".
highlights: New "Books written" stat on the death screen's gear page
Counts books you signed and contributed to the shared-book pool this run

entries.title: More lifetime stats on the all-lives death screen
entries.summary: The 'all your lives' death page now shows a grid of lifetime stat icons alongside your existing totals — hover any icon to see what it is. Tracks books written, chests opened, hearts lost, damage dealt, mobs killed, PlayerMobs killed, others come across, echos come across, and advancements earned, all summed across every life.
highlights: New 3x3 grid of hover-labelled lifetime stat icons on the all-lives page
Tracks books written, chests, hearts lost, damage dealt, mob & PlayerMob kills, passengers & echos met, and advancements earned

entries.title: Time on the train now only counts while you're alive
entries.summary: The train-time advancements (Enjoying the Ride and Marathon Passenger) previously kept ticking up while you sat at the death screen inside a carriage. Now they only accrue while you're actually alive and aboard.

entries.title: Chiseled bookshelves sometimes come stocked with books
entries.summary: Chiseled bookshelves you find around the train that don't already have a curated stash now have a chance of holding a few books of their own — and they'll visibly show the right number of books at a glance, instead of always looking empty.
highlights: Unclaimed chiseled bookshelves can now roll in with books/writable books
Bookshelf book-slot textures now correctly reflect their contents

entries.title: Anti-zombies room now needs a bit more progress to appear
entries.summary: The anti-zombies encounter room now only starts showing up once you've made a little headway into the run, instead of potentially appearing right at the start.

entries.title: Signed books remember which carriage they were written in
entries.summary: Every book and quill you sign now quietly records which carriage you were standing in at the moment of signing — laying the groundwork for future features that make use of where a story was written.
highlights: Signed books now store their carriage of origin

entries.title: "Burn without reading" book advancement
entries.summary: New milestone advancement: burn 10 starting or random books without ever opening them first. Rewards the reckless — the mirror image of the existing read-count milestones.
highlights: New hidden advancement, "Don't know, Don't wana know" — burn 10 unread books

entries.title: /bug command + logs sent with bug reports
entries.summary: Type /bug to jump straight to the bug-report question and send a report without waiting for the death screen. Reporting a bug from /bug or /feedback now attaches your recent logs so issues are far easier to diagnose.
highlights: New /bug command opens the bug report directly
Bug reports from /bug and /feedback now include your logs

entries.title: Late-game hostile gear keeps scaling past the netherite plateau
entries.summary: Past roughly 1,500 carts, hostile mobs used to stop getting tougher once their gear reached full netherite — difficulty flatlined. Now their weapons and armour keep gaining attack power and protection the deeper you ride, so long runs stay dangerous instead of plateauing. Early- and mid-game difficulty is unchanged; the new difficultyScaleHostileGearPastCap server setting (on by default) can turn it off.
highlights: Hostile gear keeps getting deadlier past ~1,500 carts, instead of capping at netherite
Early- and mid-game difficulty is untouched — only deep late-game changes
New difficultyScaleHostileGearPastCap server toggle (on by default)

entries.title: The Creator Answers advancement
entries.summary: A new advancement, "The Creator Answers", follows Summon The Creator — earned when you actually get a reply from the developer in chat.
highlights: New advancement: The Creator Answers (earned on receiving a Developer message)

entries.title: "Nothing But Books" advancement
entries.summary: New advancement: fill your entire main inventory (27 slots, not the hotbar) with story books to earn "Nothing But Books". Empty books don't count — only books with real content do.
highlights: New "Nothing But Books" advancement for filling your inventory with story books

entries.title: PlayerMob companion mod updated
entries.summary: The bundled PlayerMob (Interactive Player Mobs) companion mod is updated from 0.47.0 to 0.71.0, bringing a wave of new PlayerMob behavior and content.
highlights: PlayerMobs can now fight with TNT — rushing in, dropping a stick, and sprinting clear
Archers draw real ammo from their inventory for bows and crossbows, with fire-rate tied to their bravery
New /playermob order command to direct a PlayerMob to walk, attack, gift, steal, use items, or place blocks
Drop your own PNG skins into config/playermob/skins for PlayerMobs to wear
Optional Better Combat integration for animated melee combos

entries.title: Player-written books burn after being read
entries.summary: Books you write and sign yourself, and real books other players wrote that you discover as chest loot, now catch fire and burn away once you've read them — the same way welcome and found-in-chest story books already do.
highlights: A book you sign and keep now burns after you read it
A stranger's real book, found as loot, burns after you read it

entries.title: Pure of Heart
entries.summary: The 1,000-carriage no-damage advancement has been renamed from "Peace Train" to "Pure of Heart." A matching term has been added to the Train Glossary Entry random book for players who find one.

entries.title: Enchanted books actually enchant now
entries.summary: Books rolled as loot with a chance to be enchanted were quietly coming out as plain, unenchanted books — the enchantment was being rolled but never actually applied. Chests, barrels, and shulker boxes that carry a book entry now hand you a real Enchanted Book with a genuine random enchantment.
highlights: Book loot entries with an enchant chance now actually enchant
Fixed a couple of loot pools that were stuck using the wrong item id and could never enchant at all

entries.title: PlayerMob companion mod updated
entries.summary: The bundled PlayerMob (Interactive Player Mobs) companion mod is updated from 0.71.0 to 0.73.0, bringing new combat and death behavior.
highlights: PlayerMobs now fight with end-crystal bombing — bunkering behind a quick obsidian wall before setting one off, then retreating to do it again (toggle with /playermob endcrystalcombat)
Killing a PlayerMob now drops its full backpack, including any TNT, igniters, end crystals, and obsidian it was carrying

entries.title: Death Notes — curse a player's next life
entries.summary: Title a book & quill "Death Note", write another player's name on the first page, and sign it — the book burns away in blue soul-fire. The curse seals the moment you next die: in that player's next run, your vengeful echo lies in wait at the very carriage you fell at and hunts them down.
highlights: Sign a Death Note naming your target; it soul-burns away, and the curse arms the moment you die.
Your echo wears your skin, ignores everyone but the target, and ambushes them when they reach the carriage you died at — in any of their future runs, whatever the world.
Cut the echo down for a keepable black Death Note trophy; signing a note earns the "A Death Note" advancement.

entries.title: Find your own shared book out in the world and it tells you how it's doing
entries.summary: Stumble on a chest copy of a book you wrote and shared to the community, and it now whispers how it's been received out on the rails — how many travelers have held it, plus one telling detail: how many read it to the end, the longest anyone lingered on a single page, how many times it's been opened, how many pages have been turned, or how often someone came back to read it again. A quiet gray line, ten different ways it can read.
highlights: Holding your own community book shows how it's doing in the wild
Ten flavored variations (times read, longest read, most-lingered page, opens, page turns, re-reads)
Greets you once for each copy you find

entries.title: Death Note advancements: Self-Fulfilling Prophecy & Assassination Attempt
entries.summary: Two new advancements for the Death Note curse. Naming your own name in a Death Note now works in every build (previously dev-only) and earns Self-Fulfilling Prophecy; killing a Death Note echo hunting you earns Assassination Attempt.
highlights: New advancement: Self-Fulfilling Prophecy — write your own name in a Death Note
New advancement: Assassination Attempt — kill a Death Note echo before it kills you
You can now curse your own next life (self-targeting a Death Note works in all builds)

entries.title: Death-screen detail now feeds the community data dashboard
entries.summary: The mod now reports your full end-of-run recap — the story pages, your stats, and everything you were carrying — to the community data dashboard when you die, so it can be browsed there. No change to the death screen itself or anything else in-game.

entries.title: Deepdark stage: Warden, Wither Reality, and a new Ancient City
entries.summary: A new Deepdark stage joins the train around carriage level 87, bringing the fearsome Warden and a Wither Reality encounter, plus creepier Ancient City, Savana, and Woodstatue scenery — all built on a new set of deepdark-themed doors, floors, roofs, and walls.
highlights: New Deepdark stage (levels ~87-120)
Warden and Wither Reality enemies
Ancient City, Savana, and Woodstatue features
New deepdark carriage part set (doors/floor/roof/walls)

entries.title: Uneasy Alliance no longer earnable from Nether-band Ghasts
entries.summary: Fixed the Uneasy Alliance advancement unlocking from Ghasts encountered in the Overworld's Nether-transition zones — it now only counts a Ghast brought home to the true Overworld, matching the original intent. Thanks to ProPlayer77 for reporting this!

entries.title: Narrative books burn when taken off the lectern
entries.summary: Story letters read in place at a lectern stay safe forever, but a copy you take into your hands now burns to ash if you drop it or read it and close the book — so lecterns are the one place a story is guaranteed to keep.
highlights: Reading a story book by hand and closing it burns the book away
Dropping a narrative book burns it
Reading in place at a lectern never burns the book
